job.title: Plunger Lift Automation Service Representative
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

ChampionX (SLB) has an immediate need for a Plunger Lift Automation Service Representative located in El Reno, Oklahoma. This is your opportunity to join a global technology company, driving energy innovation for a balanced planet.

What’s in it For You:

  • The ability to make an impact and shape your career with a company that is passionate about growth.

  • The support of an organization that believes it is vital to include and engage diverse people, perspectives, and ideas to achieve our best.

  • Thrive in a company that values sustainability, drives a safety-focused culture and empowers through continuous improvement.

  • The chance to join a learning and problem-solving culture

  • Comprehensive benefits package that includes medical, dental, vision, 401(k) with company matching and more!

What You Will Do:

  • Review quotes, installation and repair plans to ensure a thorough understanding of customer needs and the parts and process required to address the need.

  • As needed, explains installation, service or repair plans, and invoices to customers; discusses cost or time overruns with team leader or manager.

  • Executes installations, repair, or service plans and documents results.

  • Creates product failure analysis reports.

  • Develops good working relationships with customers through professional communication.

  • Develop and maintain application competency in Production Automation. Automation competencies include computer skills in SCADA, telemetry, Windows operating environment, database technologies, Internet, and networking.  Wireless communication competency required (SCADA, telemetry, host systems, etc.).

  • Ensure an accurate account of all truck inventory.

  • Knows and complies with all safety policies and procedures.

  • Ability to work weekends and overtime as required.

  • Other duties as required.

Minimum Experience:

  • Four years of relevant experience in the oil and gas industry.

  • Valid driver's license

  • Strong mechanical skills - Perform mechanical field maintenance work to ensure optimal availability and reliability of artificial lift equipment.

  • Good oral and written communication skills - Listens to understand input, feedback and concerns. Responds timely and effective.

  • Good analytical and problem-solving skills - Identifies and resolves problems. Develops alternative solutions; Works well in group problem solving situations; Uses reason even when dealing with emotional topics.

  • Good math skills

  • Good organizational and time management skills, with the ability to work with minimal supervision - Prioritizes and plans work activities; Uses time efficiently; Plans for additional resources

  • Good computer skills, including Microsoft Office suite

  • Immigration sponsorship not offered for this role

Pre-Employment Requirements:

  • Motor vehicle record check

  • Drug and alcohol test

  • Criminal background check

Physical Demands:

  • Role is deemed safety-sensitive and may be subject to employer or customer drug testing.

  • Must be able to lift and carry 80 lbs. regularly, and 100 lbs. occasionally.

  • Specific vision abilities required by this job include close vision, distance vision, peripheral vision, depth perception and ability to adjust focus.

  • The employee is regularly required to talk or hear.

  • The employee is frequently required to use hands to finger, handle, or feel and reach with hands and arms.

  • Employee is required to occasionally walk which may include inclines, uneven surfaces, and a variety of weather conditions.

  • Employee must be able to regularly bend, stoop, crawl, lay down, twist and push.

  • Employee is regularly exposed to work near moving mechanical parts; outdoor weather conditions; extreme cold (non-weather) and extreme heat (non-weather).

  • Employee is required to wear appropriate personal protective equipment including but not limited to shoes/boots, hard hats, or respirators.

  • Employee is frequently exposed to wet or humid conditions (non-weather); fumes or airborne particles; toxic or caustic chemicals; risk of electrical shock and work with explosives.

  • Employee is occasionally exposed to work in high, precarious places and vibration.

  • Employee is exposed to loud noise level in the work environment.

job.title: Sales Account Manager - Artificial Lift
job.description:
SLB is seeking a Technical Software Sales Representative

responsible for executing the regional sales strategy for the Texas Region.

What’s in it For You:

  • You will join a growing company offering competitive pay and benefits

  • Access to best-in-class resources, tools, and technology

  • Opportunity for a long term, advanced career path

  • A culture that values safety first, including training and personal protection

Key Responsibilities

  • Work closely with current and new customers to understand business needs and recommend solutions that assist the customer in meeting their productivity objectives, and improvements that will maintain and grow sales within assigned territory.

  • Partner with Software Engineers / Developers to deliver technical presentation that provide solutions to the customers through XSPOC or other software solutions.

  • Engage in problem solving by performing system analysis, interpreting data and providing written recommendations to ensure customer operations are performing at optimal levels.

  • Actively sell and support software innovations and technology in assigned customers to promote long-term business relationships with SLB.

  • Assist in developing strong relationships with key stakeholders in current and new customers, including plant or facility executives

  • Provide technical support to customers; identifying and resolving customer complaints, escalating as required.

  • Support sales forecasting and market data driven sales strategy.

  • Maintain and updates all sales activity data through Salesforce.com.

  • Mentor other Sales Representatives and Customer Sales Engineers, with a career interest in leading teams.  

Skills/Knowledge:

  • Strong sales network within software for artificial lift, with a proven success in acquiring new customers.

  • Strategic mindset, with the ability to develop sales strategies, analytical and implementation skills.

  • Strong communication, interpersonal and organizational skills including the ability to present and persuade at the leadership level.

  • Excellent negotiation skills.

  • Competitive attitude and strong work ethic with the ability to enthusiastically represent the company.

  • Experience and Knowledge of Automation, Enterprise Software and IIOT for the Oil & Gas industry.

  • Knowledge of Artificial Lift and Well Optimization

  • Familiarity with Oil and Gas control and measurement systems

  • Familiarity with Variable Frequency Drive/Variable Speed Drive integration and control

  • Self-driven motivated

  • Available for inter-regional travel up to 50%.

Minimum Qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s degree

  • 2 years sales experience in Oil & Gas segment with strategic connections

  • Preferred software sales experience for artificial lift system sales experience

  • Strong negational skills

  • Ability to develop effective business relationships

  • Exceptional verbal, written, and presentation skills.

  • Ability to work effectively both independently and as part of a team.

  • Salesforce (or other CRM) Experience and Oracle.

  • Availability for inter-regional travel 50%.

job.title: Shop Technician - Gas Lift
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

We are looking for a Gas Lift Shop Technician located in Odessa, TX. This is your opportunity to join a global technology company, driving energy innovation for a balanced planet.

What’s In It For You:

  • First shift generally starts at 5:00 am and may be 8, 10 or 12 hours depending on the workload (with some weekend overtime as needed)

  • Thrive in a company that values safety and continuous improvement and encourages career growth

  • Comprehensive benefits package that includes medical, dental, vision, 401(k) with company matching and more!

What You Will Do:

  • Attend to breakdowns, diagnose faults, and oversee equipment improvements and time critical repairs; maintain preventative maintenance and breakdown report and part usage records; investigate the root cause and recommend course of action.

  • Assists shop on problem identification, trouble shooting and equipment issue resolution and repair. Evaluate non-conforming products to determine appropriate requirements to rework, repair, or scrap items.

  • Oversee spare parts inventory and may use a computerized maintenance management system.

  • Coordinate and direct contractors for specialized repairs and installations; maintain good working relations with internal customers and contractors.

  • Maintain toolkit, supplies, and requisite tool inventory.

  • Conduct facility and equipment inspections as a part of the Emergency Response Procedures.

  • Lead and implement Lean 5S + 1 project as a role model.

  • Know and comply with all safety policies and procedures.

  • Utilize hand or power tools and heavy equipment, such as, forklifts and pallet jacks in a safe, reliable manner.

  • Assist with inventory counts of current stock levels to identify potential shortages or excesses.

  • Complete inspections, repair of parts and routine maintenance on capillary trailers.

  • Deliver parts to technicians on job sites.

Pre-Employment requirements

  • Drug screen

  • Background Check

  • Motor Vehicle Check

Minimum Qualifications:  

  • High school diploma or GED

  • 2 years of mechanical shop experience

  • Valid drivers license

  • This safety-sensitive role may be subject to employer or customer drug testing

  • Read, understand and speak English

  • Must be willing to work overtime and Saturdays as required


 

Physical Demands:

  • The physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job.

  • Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.

  • Medium work:  Exerting up to 50 pounds of force occasionally and/or up to 20 pounds of force frequently, and/or up to 10 pounds of force constantly to move objects.

  • Requires Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) such as safety shoes and safety glasses

  • May stand, walk, stoop, sit, bend, twist, grasp, lift, climb,  

  • Requires visual and hearing acuity

  • Work primarily occurs in a heated and air-conditioned manufacturing environment

  • Hazards include proximity to moving mechanical parts, moving vehicles, electrical current or exposure to chemicals

  • Atmospheric conditions that affect the respiratory system or skin may include fumes, odors, dust, mists, gases, or poor ventilation

  • Potential skin exposure to oils and other cutting fluids

job.title: Gas Lift Service Technician
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

We are looking for a Gas Lift Service Technician located in Tyler, TX, supporting PCS Ferguson Gas Lift product line. This is your opportunity to join a global technology company, driving energy innovation for a balanced planet.

What’s in it For You:

  • Earn Field Job Bonus Opportunity

  • The support of an organization that believes it is vital to include and engage diverse people, perspectives, and ideas to achieve our best

  • Thrive in a company that values sustainability, drives a safety-focused culture and has been recognized as one of the most ethical companies in the world

  • Comprehensive benefits package starting day 1 of employment- medical, dental, vision, matching 401k, and more!

What You Will Do:

  • Provides technical support for the gas lift product line.

  • Reviews quotes, installation and repair plans to ensure a thorough understanding of customer needs and the parts and process required to address the need.

  • As needed, explains installation, service or repair plans, and invoices to customers; discusses cost or time overruns with team leader or manager.

  • Executes installations, repair, or service plans and documents results.

  • Creates product failure analysis reports.

  • Develops good working relationships with customers through professional communication.

  • Operates, recommends and maintains packer and gas lift products and services to meet customer need. This includes, but is not limited to, operating various styles of packers, including assembling, troubleshooting, and releasing of packers.

  • Comprehends a tubing tally and properly fills out installation reports for our customers.

  • Executes tubing set and wireline set packers to company expectations.

  • Performs initial completions, remedial, or downhole maintenance operations from an elevated position.

  • Maintains and performs preventative maintenance and basic repairs to workover rigs and associated equipment, checks fluid, oil, water, hydraulic, and transmission levels.

  • Understands and uses various completion tools complementing the packer product line.

  • Coordinate’s assignments or projects to achieve maximum utilization of clients’ drilling and oil recovery efforts.

  • Work independently at remote customer locations.

  • Troubleshoots and make recommendations for well flow enhancement.

Pre-Employment Requirements:

  • Motor vehicle record check

  • Drug and alcohol test

  • Criminal background check

  • Education and certification check

Minimum Qualifications:

  • High school diploma/GED

  • 2 years’ equivalent field tech work experience

  • Good technical/mechanical aptitude

  • Strong troubleshooting skills

  • Excellent customer service skills

  • Valid driver's license in state of residence required

  • 21 years of age to operate company vehicle

Physical Demands:

  • Must be able to lift and carry 50 lbs regularly, and 100 lbs occasionally.

  • Specific vision abilities required by this job include close vision, distance vision, peripheral vision, depth perception and ability to adjust focus.

  • The employee is regularly required to talk or hear.

  • The employee is frequently required to use hands to finger, handle, or feel and reach with hands and arms.

  • Employee is required to occasionally walk in customer field locations which may include inclines, uneven surfaces, and a variety of weather conditions.

  • Employee must be able to regularly bend, stoop, crawl, lay down, twist and push.

  • Employee is required to wear appropriate personal protective equipment including but not limited to shoes/boots, hard hats, or respirators.

job.title: Assistant Plant Operator
job.description:
Disclaimer

SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

About the Role

ChampionX has an immediate need for an Assistant Plant Operator in our Chemical Technologies group located in Odessa, TX. This position will join the production team in the Odessa plant and allow you to learn and progress through all operational skills required to blend, react, and package products within the facility. The role reports to the Production Team Leads and works closely with other core functional groups within the Odessa ChampionX Manufacturing Plant.

If you are a dedicated professional with a proven track record of success, we invite you to apply. This is your opportunity to join a large growing company offering competitive base rates and comprehensive benefits.

What’s in it for You

  • Access to best-in-class resources, tools, technology, and continuous learning opportunities
  • Thrive in a company that values a culture of safety, including industry-leading safety training and personal protective equipment
  • The opportunity to make an impact and shape your career with a company that is passionate about growth
  • Support from an organization that believes diverse people, perspectives, and ideas drive better results
  • Pride in working for a company focused on clean water, safe food, abundant energy, and healthy environments
  • Comprehensive benefits package starting on Day 1, including medical, dental, vision, 401(k) with company match, and more

What You Will Do

  • Complete work functions within production operations on a daily basis
  • Responsible for quality blending of finished products and the bulk, drum, and packaging of products
  • Perform general forklift duties as required
  • Operate mechanical and electrical equipment including pumps, mixing equipment, and automation control systems used in blending and packaging operations
  • Complete and comply with all safety initiatives and certification requirements, including near-miss and incident reporting
  • Accountable for data entry of production tickets, goods receipts, and shipping documentation
  • Conduct daily inspections of work areas and complete monthly reporting requirements
  • Maintain daily housekeeping standards to ensure a clean and organized work environment at all times
  • Assist with physical inventory counts and inventory processes
  • Ensure compliance with all applicable ISO requirements

Position Details

  • Location: Odessa, TX
  • Department: Chemical Technologies Manufacturing Operations
  • Reports To: Production Team Leads
  • Schedule: Plant Operations Schedule
  • Role is deemed safety-sensitive and may be subject to employer or customer drug testing

Minimum Qualifications

  • High School Diploma or GED
  • Must be medically qualified to wear a respirator
  • Physically able to lift up to 60 pounds, climb stairs and ladders, and perform squatting and bending activities (Agility Tests 1, 2a & 2b)
  • Immigration sponsorship is not offered for this role

Preferred Qualifications

  • Team player with the ability to perform physical work in a manufacturing environment
  • Basic computer skills with the ability to navigate core business programs
  • Two-year Process Technology degree preferred
  • Minimum of one (1) year of experience as an operator in a batch or specialty chemical manufacturing plant
  • Minimum of one (1) year of relevant manufacturing or production experience
  • Forklift operation certification preferred
  • 24-Hour HAZWOPER certification preferred

Physical Demands

  • Ability to lift, push, pull, and carry up to 60 pounds
  • Ability to climb stairs and ladders regularly
  • Ability to bend, squat, kneel, and perform repetitive physical activities
  • Must be able to wear required personal protective equipment, including a respirator

ChampionX is now part of SLB, a global technology company driving energy innovation for a balanced planet. As innovators, that has been our mission for nearly a century.

We are a technology company that unlocks access to energy for the benefit of all. Today, we face a global imperative to create a future with more energy and less carbon. job.title: Chemist
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

ChampionX has a need for Chemist located in KL, Malaysia. This is your opportunity to join a growing company offering a competitive base salary and benefits.

As a Chemist at ChampionX, you will play a crucial role in the development, optimization and application of production chemical solutions to Oil and Gas customers. You will be responsible for running technical projects, conducting research, development, and coming up with technical solutions to address our customer’s challenges. You will work on projects both in the laboratory and in the field. As part of the role, you may be required to travel to different sites to conduct testing or meet with internal or external customers to discuss technical topics. The role will also allow the opportunity to present novel research or technical developments in conferences or industry events.

What You Will Do:
• Assist with Technical Support, Product Development and Selection.
• Conduct laboratory testing to develop, select or evaluate product performance, secondary properties and compatibilities. Prepare technical reports on the completed lab work.
• Support field-based projects. This may include troubleshooting of chemical related issues, conducting product selection and evaluation, and/or executing field trials.
• Collaborate with cross-functional teams regionally and globally on technical projects, roll-out new developments and/or optimize internal processes.
• Implement and follow the company’s safety policy (Goal Zero) and procedures. Update laboratory test procedures and safety related documents where necessary.
• Support the maintenance of the day-to-day operations in the laboratory. Ensure equipment is calibrated and maintained.
• Take a professional approach to self-development. Identify appropriate training opportunities.
• Any other task that may reasonably be requested.

Desired Competencies:
• Readily learns and adopts new technologies
• Asks the right questions to analyse situations
• Takes on challenges of unfamiliar tasks and learns from experiences
 

Education and Experience:
Degree or Advanced degree in Chemistry, Chemical Engineering or related discipline.
Job Requirements:
• Primarily laboratory and office based
• Field work will be required
• Ability to travel overseas

Desirable Education and Experience:
• Previous experience of working with oilfield chemical related projects
• Previous experience of working in a laboratory
• Previous experience of managing day-to-day projects
• Good computer skills, must have experience in Microsoft Word, Excel, PowerPoint
• Good oral and written communication skills, speak and write English


Physical Requirements:
May require long periods of standing conducting experiments in a laboratory or travel to visit the field

About ChampionX:
ChampionX, provides specialty chemistry programs and related services for upstream and midstream oil and gas operations looking to maximize production, protect assets and reduce operating costs and environmental impact. Through on-site problem solving and innovative technologies, our team provides solutions that help energy customers overcome complex challenges in the world’s toughest energy frontiers.

Our Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion 

We believe the best teams are diverse and inclusive, and we are on a journey to create a workplace where every employee can grow and achieve their best. We are committed to fair and equal treatment of employees and applicants. We recruit, hire, promote, transfer and provide opportunities for advancement on the basis of individual qualifications and job performance. job.title: Production Planner
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

ChampionX has future and immediate hiring needs for experienced Production Planner I professionals in Evansville, WY to support our operations team. This is your opportunity to join a large growing company offering competitive compensation and benefits.

What’s in it For You:

  • Access to best-in-class resources, tools, technology and continuous education
  • Thrive in a company that values a culture of safety to include top-notch safety training and personal protection equipment
  • Pride in working for a company that provides clean water, safe food, abundant energy and healthy environments
  • Comprehensive benefits package that includes medical, dental, vision, matching 401(k) with company matching and more!

What You Will Do:

  • Run MRP efficiently and schedule production to meet customer order due dates.
  • Develop and maintain daily, weekly, and monthly production plans to ensure customer service levels, minimize production costs, and optimize product sequencing.
  • Review the hot sheet daily, update plant remarks, and apply late codes as needed to drive key performance metrics such as SIFOT, Reliability, Responsiveness, and Overall Performance.
  • Participate in alignment meetings with critical cross-functional teams to ensure schedule accuracy and operational efficiency.
  • Provide daily status updates to internal and external stakeholders regarding open order exceptions, recommending re-planning actions to minimize non-conformances.
  • Identify and implement cost-reduction and availability improvement initiatives.
  • Review process orders to confirm raw material availability prior to releasing to production.
  • Schedule bulk orders to meet on-time loading requirements.
  • Manage batch adjustments, including cancellations, reschedules, and modifications as needed.
  • Enter repacks and relabels for customer-specific requirements.
  • Collaborate with the raw material planner to ensure timely material availability.
  • Lead scheduling for new product introductions and break-in batches.
  • Communicate daily with the production team on critical priorities, especially bulk loading needs.
  • Coordinate with tactical planning to prioritize schedules and meet customer commitments.
  • Work closely with the inventory team to resolve discrepancies and maintain accurate stock levels.
  • Adhere to all safety policies, supporting SLB’s Goal Zero principles to continually improve safety, health, environmental performance, and compliance.
  • Promote a culture of safety by reporting observations and incidents promptly.
  • Support and uphold the Quality Improvement Process, ensuring error-free work and a “do it right the first time” mindset.
  • Identify and drive process improvement opportunities within the planning function to support continuous improvement initiatives.
  • Resolves variances in inventory, conducts investigations and reports corrections of inventory to the Plant Manager.
  • Daily Review of all Process Orders for completeness and accuracy.
  • Generate Cycle Count Sheets, Freeze Inventory, and Create Inventory Documents in SAP.
  • Provides inventory SAP support for Materials Movement, Operations, and Administrative staff.
  • Provides daily, weekly, and monthly reporting as required.
  • Clear SAP System Database error logs by conducting investigations and makes appropriate corrections.
  • Process and schedule New Orders/Shortages
  • Other various tasks connected incoming materials and production scheduling

Position Details:

  • Ability to work in a fast-paced manufacturing and production environment
  • Ability to manage multiple priorities and daily schedule changes
  • Role may require collaboration across production, inventory, and supply chain teams
  • Role is deemed safety-sensitive and may be subject to employer or customer drug testing.

Minimum Qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Business, Supply Chain Management, Data Science, or a related subject
  • 2 years of relevant experience or an additional 4 years of experience in lieu of Bachelor’s degree
  • Knowledge of Scheduling & ERP systems, such as FST, and SAP
  • Working knowledge of Microsoft Excel
  • Good at managing workloads and flexible with daily changes
  • Understanding the complexities of the supply chain and how to maneuver through the challenges
  • Good written and oral communication skills
  • Experience with SAP, FST, and Warehouse Management
  • Background in Production Scheduling
  • Basic knowledge of plant operations

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Experience supporting production planning and scheduling activities in a manufacturing environment
  • Experience with inventory management and warehouse operations
  • Strong organizational and problem-solving skills
  • Prior experience that demonstrates strong ethics, effective time management, ability to multitask, adaptability, autonomy, and self-motivation
  • High computer application literacy (including Microsoft Office Suite, and ability to learn internal business systems)
  • Experience driving process improvements and operational efficiencies

Physical Demands:

  • May be required to Lift/Push/Pull/Carry up to 55 pounds chest high.
  • Role is deemed safety-sensitive and may be subject to employer or customer drug testing.

About Us

ChampionX is now part of SLB, a global technology company driving energy innovation for a balanced planet. As innovators, that’s been our mission for nearly a century. We are a technology company that unlocks access to energy for the benefit of all. As innovators, that’s been our mission for nearly a century. Today, we face a global imperative to create a future
with more energy, but less carbon. Our diverse, innovative change makers are focused on going further in innovation and inventing the new energy technologies we need to get there. job.title: Water Treatment / Frac Specialist
job.description:
Job Posting Description

Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

We are looking for a Water Treatment / Frac Specialist located in Hobbs, New Mexico. This is your opportunity to join a global technology company, driving energy innovation for a balanced planet.

What’s In It For You:

  • You will join a growing company offering competitive pay and benefits

  • Access to best-in-class resources, tools, and technology

  • Opportunity for a long term, advanced career path

  • A culture that values safety first, including training and personal protection

What You Will Do:

  • Provide the administration of chemicals to treat various water conditions on Frac locations

  • Load trailers and schedule runs to replenish inventory on your location

  • Operate Frac trailer designed to inject chemical into water transfer lines

  • Act as an ambassador with customers to showcase value to our customers

  • Follow a rotating schedule

  • Adhere to company policies and follow Standard Operating Procedure per job task

  • Wear required personal protection equipment (PPE) at all times during job tasks

  • Always maintain equipment in a safe and operable condition at all times by performing daily and periodic mechanical and safety inspections

  • Responsible for navigating via “oilfield directions” safely to and from job sites.

  • Maintain regular communication with sales and supervisor in order to report field problems and/or safety hazards

  • Wear appropriate safety gear at all times during operations

  • Keeps current on safety techniques and take advantage of all opportunities to improve driving and safety skills

  • Recognize and avoid safety hazards in the work environment

Pre-Employment Requirements:

  • Acceptable motor vehicle record

  • Must be able to pass a drug screen and physical exam

  • Must be able to pass Pulmonary Function Test & wear respirator

Position Details:

  • Rotational Shift 14 / 7

Minimum Qualifications:

  • Minimum high school graduate or GED

  • Have a current Class C Driver's License, issued by the state in which the candidate resides

  • Acceptable motor vehicle record (3 years)

  • Immigration sponsorship not offered for this role

Physical Demands:

  • Role is deemed safety-sensitive and may be subject to employer or customer drug testing

  • Must be able to pass a drug screen and physical exam

  • Must be able to pass Pulmonary Function Test & wear respirator

  • Participate in annual respirator program (clean shaved)

  • Be able to lift and carry a minimum of 50lbs

  • Travel to other locations as needs

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Experience with the operation of forklifts, drum dollies, trailer hoists, chemical pumps, hoses, meters, printers and gauges

  • Experience with handling products in various types of packages including bulk, drums, Stainless Steel totes or IBCs

  • Experience in customer relations

  • Experience performing warehouse duties, which include preventative maintenance duties, inventory and ordering of chemicals, receiving incoming shipments, organizing warehouse for safe and efficient chemical storage, operation of customized chemical manufacturing equipment and the upkeep of warehouse grounds

  • Awareness of chemical safety and the measures to take in the event of skin contact or spill

  • Mechanically inclined

job.title: Regional Sales Manager - South Texas Region
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

ChampionX (SLB) has an immediate need for a Regional Sales Manager - South Texas Region. This person will need to be within located a 60 mile radius of Kennedy, TX. This is your opportunity to join a global technology company, driving energy innovation for a balanced planet.

What’s in it For You:

  • The ability to make an impact and shape your career with a company that is passionate about growth.

  • The support of an organization that believes it is vital to include and engage diverse people, perspectives, and ideas to achieve our best.

  • Thrive in a company that values sustainability, drives a safety-focused culture and empowers through continuous improvement.

  • The chance to join a learning and problem-solving culture

  • Comprehensive benefits package that includes medical, dental, vision, 401(k) with company matching and more!

What You Will Do:

Sales Strategy and Planning

  • Effectively develop and execute strategic regional sales plans for chemical injection pumps within the South Texas Region, with focus on the Kennedy, Texas territory
  • Evaluate and implement appropriate sales techniques to support the strategic growth objectives while maintaining a profitable gross margin & repeatable sales revenues
  • Manage sales, profit margins and operational expenses for designated sales territory
  • Develop and update reliable sales forecasts
  • Identify, qualify, and convert project and new customer sales opportunities that will contribute to the territory revenues
  • Develop install base in adjacent segments to diversify market share in other regions outside of O&G
  • Provide periodic sales activity report

Customer Relationship Management

  • Set-up customer appointments to conduct sales presentations to promote, sell, and apply our complete product line in a regional sales area
  • Manage assigned accounts as well as developing new accounts, projects, or other sales opportunities
  • Provide product training at the field and corporate levels for all customer types
  • Provide customers with technical support and expertise in reviewing specifications and applying the products
  • Attend relevant product conventions, sales seminars, and tradeshows

Market Analysis

  • Develop annual marketing strategy in conjunction with the Director of Sales and the inside sales team
  • Identify joint marketing opportunities where applicable
  • Perform market assessments, competitor analyses, pricing strategies and execute counter strategies to gain market share
  • Draw conclusions from market assessment data on the competitive environment and the company’s strengths, weaknesses, opportunities, and targets

Collaboration

  • Develop relationships with internal sales teams, leverage synergies and drive joint sales opportunities
  • Collaborate and provide performance feedback to Manufacture Representatives/Distributors for the area of responsibility
  • Communicate with the Sales Team and the Production Team regularly to guarantee that sales and client objectives are met

Position Details:

  • Candidate must reside in San Antonio, TX or surrounding areas
  • Route territory will be the South Texas Region
  • Compensation package offered: Competitive base salary with eligibility for a quarterly sales incentive bonus plan
  • New hire product training, new hire onboarding and orientation provided
  • Plant visits will consist of office and cubicle environments in a manufacturing facility with PPE requirements
  • Immigration is not offered for this position

Minimum Requirements:

  • Bachelor’s degree in mechanical engineering, business, or equivalent experience.
  • 3 – 5 years of experience in customer-facing, outside sales of oil and gas
  • Willingness to travel up to 50-75% in the specified sales territory
  • Contract negotiation experience
  • Experience creating and presenting complex sales reports

Soft Skills:

  • Adapt at managing multiple tasks simultaneously
  • Results Oriented - Good follow up skills to ensure strategy implementation
  • Provide leadership through example and adequate direction
  • Able to communicate with customers at all levels
  • Able to build relationships with company and customer contacts
  • Professional and positive attitude
  • Passion for continuous improvement
  • Open to change and new information
  • Promote collaboration and share best practices towards the larger strategic business goals.

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Experience working with chemical injection pumps
  • Experience with Automation
  • Technical expertise in oil and gas chemical injections pumps
  • Knowledge of Wellmark & Timberline products

​Physical Demands:

  • Role is deemed safety-sensitive and may be subject to employer or customer drug testing.
  • Minimal climbing stairs, lifting between 10lbs up to 25lbs, handling, grasping, turning, holding or using hands, dialing, standing, walking, hearing and writing
  • Occasional speaking between 11% and 33%
  • Constant use of a keyboard, mouse and viewing a computer monitor, sitting, and seeing greater than 66%

job.title: EOR Chemist
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

We are seeking a Chemist for our Chemically Enhanced Oil Recovery (CEOR) Technology team located in Aberdeen, UK to support both business and customer needs.  The Chemist will be responsible for supporting more senior team members with lab testing, managing basic customer support projects, and supporting a safe and efficient laboratory.  The ideal candidate is versatile, communicates effectively, and thrives in a collaborative environment. If you are a passionate professional that likes to work in an autonomous, hands-on environment, we invite you to apply.

What’s in it For You:

  • The ability to make an impact and shape your career with a company that’s passionate about growth.
  • The support of an organization that believes it’s vital to include and engage diverse people, perspectives, and ideas to achieve our best.
  • The opportunity to thrive in a company that values sustainability, drives a safety-focused culture, and empowers its employees through continuous improvement.
  • Flexible working arrangements and a comprehensive benefits package starting on day one of employment, including medical, dental, vision, matching 401k, and more!

What You Will Do:

  • Apply basic chemistry principles, theories and concepts to assignments where precedents typically exist
  • Use basic scientific knowledge to solve problems
  • Contribute to the research and development of differentiated technology, products, processes and systems.
  • Perform work in a safe manner while identifying opportunities for improving safety
  • Manage several requests at once while working within a fast-paced environment

Minimum Qualifications:

  • Bachelor of Science in Chemistry, Physics, Biology, Biochemistry, or related field
  • Immigration Sponsorship is not available for this role.

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Experience working with polymers and/or surfactants
  • Experience using a viscometer/rheometer, preparing samples in a glove box, using a coreflood, and running contact angle/IFT measurements
  • Excellent written and oral communication skills and the ability to communicate within all levels of the organization
  • The candidate should be outgoing, creative, a self-starter, and team oriented

About ChampionX

ChampionX is a global leader in providing chemistry programs and services, drilling technology, artificial lift solutions, and automation technologies for the upstream and midstream oil and gas industry. Our world-class safety culture fuels our purpose to improve lives through our commitment to deliver sustainable operations.

job.description:

The ability to make an impact and shape your career with a company that’s passionate about growth.

Position Details:

  • Performing onsite customer support activities for chemical injection pumps and tanks, such
  • Install, troubleshoot, repair and set ups pump equipment

job.title: Field Operations Manager
job.description:
Job Description

Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with

ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

The Field Operations Manager is responsible for overseeing transportation, logistics, and field operations to ensure efficient, cost-effective, and reliable service delivery. This role manages route planning, resource allocation, and supply chain coordination while maintaining control of operational expenses and ensuring alignment with customer requirements. The position serves as a key point of contact for clients, supporting account management and ensuring clear, responsive communication.

In addition to technical responsibilities, the Field Operations Manager leads a team of 10–30 employees across multiple shifts, driving performance through strong leadership, training, and ongoing coaching. This role emphasizes maintaining high standards for safety, compliance, and operational excellence, including auditing field practices and reinforcing safe work behaviors. The manager is also responsible for fostering team morale, addressing challenges, and promoting a positive work environment. Success in this role requires adaptability, strong organizational and problem-solving skills, and the ability to balance multiple priorities in a dynamic, fast-paced environment.

Minimum Education & Experience Requirements

  • Prior supervisory or people-leadership experience
  • Willingness and ability to travel as required
  • Valid driver’s license with an acceptable Motor Vehicle Record

Required Skills & Experience (Must – Haves)

  • Minimum of 5 years of experience in fleet, transportation, or logistics within the Oil & Gas services industry, third-party logistics, or a related field
  • At least 3 years of demonstrated supervisory or leadership experience
  • Ability and willingness to travel as needed to support business operations
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite and the ability to quickly learn and navigate internal business systems
  • Proven track record of professional ethics, effective time management, multitasking, adaptability, and self-motivation
  • Demonstrated excellent communication and organizational skills, with the ability to perform effectively in a fast-paced environment
  • Strong negotiation, decision-making, and problem-resolution capabilities
  • Valid driver’s license with an acceptable Motor Vehicle Record (MVR)
  • This position is not eligible for employment-based immigration sponsorship

Preferred Skills & Experience (Nice to Have)

  • Experience with related chemicals and logistics. routing, or third-party logistics
  • Oilfield experience
  • Bachelor’s degree or equivalent combination of education and experience
  • Knowledge of route optimization and load scheduling best practices
  • Experience managing budgets, OPEX, and cost controls
  • Strong negotiation skills and experience working directly with customers

Job Duties:

  • Develop and optimize route planning and load scheduling for multi-stop deliveries to ensure efficiency and service reliability
  • Manage and monitor monthly operating expenses (OPEX) to maintain cost control and financial performance
  • Oversee and direct all transportation operations to ensure safe, timely, and compliant service delivery
  • Allocate, track, and document resources and operational movements to support accurate reporting and planning
  • Manage customer accounts, ensuring service expectations are met and strong relationships are maintained
  • Coordinate delivery scheduling through MRP systems and serve as a liaison between operations and customers
  • Ensure effective collaboration across the supply chain to maintain seamless and efficient operational workflows

About ChampionX

ChampionX is now part of SLB, a global technology company driving energy innovation for a

balanced planet. As innovators, that’s been our mission for nearly a century.

We are a technology company that unlocks access to energy for the benefit of all. As innovators,

that’s been our mission for nearly a century. Today, we face a global imperative to create a future

with more energy, but less carbon. Our diverse, innovative change makers are focused on going

further in innovation and inventing the new energy technologies we need to get there.

For more news and information, visit SLB.com

job.title: Journeyperson Pipefitter
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

We are looking for a Journeyperson Pipefitter located in Rockyview, AB. If you are a dedicated professional with a proven track record of technical trades work and someone who likes to work in a fast-paced, hands-on environment, we invite you to apply. This is your opportunity to join a large growing company offering a competitive base salary and benefits. 

What’s in it For You:

  • Flexible, independent work environment where you will manage a monthly schedule

  • Access to best-in-class resources, tools, and technology

  • Grow your income as you drive growth

  • Thrive in a company that values a culture of safety, to include top-notch safety training and personal protection equipment

  • Comprehensive benefits package that includes medical, dental, vision, a company-paid pension plan, stock purchase plan, tuition reimbursement and more!

​What You Will Do:

  • Read and interpret P&IDs, isometric drawings, and other technical drawings

  • Lay out, assemble, install, and maintain piping systems for industrial skid packages

  • Perform bolt-up and threaded pipe installations in accordance with specifications

  • Complete hydrostatic testing (hydrotests) of piping systems

  • Participate in and support crane and rigging operations

  • Measure, cut, fit, and align piping components

  • Ensure installations meet applicable codes, standards, and quality requirements

  • Work closely with fabrication, welding, and electrical teams to complete assemblies

  • Assist with material take-offs, job planning, and workflow coordination

Position Details:

  • Plant located in Rockyview, AB (North of Calgary).

  • Candidate resides in Calgary, AB or nearby.

  • Work week and shift: 4 days/ week (10-hour shift).

  • Immigration sponsorship not available for this position.

Minimum Qualifications:

  • Journeyman Pipefitter Certificate (Alberta recognized or Red Seal)

  • Experience in industrial piping fabrication and installation

  • Ability to read and interpret P&IDs and ISO drawings

  • Experience with hydrotesting, rigging, and working around cranes

  • Familiarity with threaded and bolted piping systems

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Strong attention to detail and quality workmanship

  • Effective time management and ability to work independently or as part of a team.

  • Experience working on skid-mounted process equipment

Physical Demands:

  • Must have the ability to Lift/Push/Pull/Carry up to 55 pounds chest high.

  • Ability to work in shop and outdoor environments

  • This is a safety-sensitive position, and the successful candidate will be required to complete drug and alcohol testing.

job.title: Material Handler- Paint Vat
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration. We are looking for a Material Handler-Paint Vat located in Tulsa, OK. This is your opportunity to join a global technology company, driving energy innovation for a balanced planet.

What’s in it For You:

  • The support of an organization that believes it is vital to include and engage diverse people, perspectives, and ideas to achieve our best

  • Thrive in a company that values sustainability, drives a safety-focused culture, and empowers through continuous improvement

What You Will Do:

  • Operates equipment in a safe efficient manner to avoid spills or damage to equipment or products being transported.

  • Understands mechanics of  assigned unit.

  • Ensures that material is properly stacked or tiered.

  • Operates chop saw for test bars. Moves material to location designated on material tag.

  • Has knowledge of all materials, whether in process of manufacture or finished, used in assigned department.

  • Checks hoist, spreader, and spreader chains daily. Hooks and unhooks material for crane operator.

  • Keeps machines stocked with material. Moves material to forwarding zone and tags it for other departments. Keeps spacer bars in orderly condition.

  • Constantly observes material for rust and straightness.

  • Makes written reports to supervisor as to heats and condition of material, regarding defective materials and mixed heats. 

  • Capable of safely and efficiently driving any type of truck, yard truck crane, or truck-tractor with trailer or winch.

  • Has good knowledge of proper and safe use of binders, chains, winch lines, snatch blocks, side booms, or gin poles.

  • Responsible for seeing that assigned unit or equipment is not neglected or abused.

  • Reports all needed repairs to supervisor.

  • Helps load, unload and check any material which is received or shipped by company.

  • Maintains clean and orderly work area.

Minimum Qualifications:

  • Two year technical degree or equivalent education and/or experience. Valid driver's license in state of residence required.

Physical Demands:  

  • Must be able to lift and carry 50 lbs regularly, and 100 lbs occasionally.

  • Specific vision abilities required by this job include close vision, distance vision, peripheral vision, depth perception and ability to adjust focus.

  • The employee is regularly required to talk or hear.

  • The employee is frequently required to use hands to finger, handle, or feel and reach with hands and arms.

  • Employee is required to occasionally walk in customer field locations which may include inclines, uneven surfaces, and a variety of weather conditions.

  • Employee must be able to regularly bend, stoop, crawl, lay down, twist and push.

job.title: Maintenance Worker
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

We are looking for a Maintenance Worker located in West Valley City, UT. This is your opportunity to join a global technology company, driving energy innovation for a balanced planet.

What’s in it For You:

  • Join a growing company offering competitive pay
  • Comprehensive benefits package that includes medical, dental, vision, 401(k) with company matching and more
  • Tackle some of the world’s most meaningful challenges including, helping customers achieve clean water, safe food, abundant energy, and healthy environments
  • The ability to shape your career with a company that is passionate about growth
  • Be part of a culture that values its employees and puts safety first
  • Grow your income as you drive growth
  • Opportunity for a long term, advanced career path
  • Access to Employee Resource Groups (ERG’s). These employee-led groups allow employees to come together to explore common interests and foster a diverse and inclusive workplace
  • Access to best-in-class resources, tools, and technology

What You Will Do:

  • Support and engage in our vision of improving lives 
  • Live and be an example of Quartzdyne’s core values: Respect, Grit, Transparency, Honesty, Humility and Accountability 
  • Be a disciple and leader of continuous improvement

Position Details:

  • Performs a wide range of mechanical and electrical maintenance tasks
  • Uses hand and power tools to accomplish these tasks
  • Light repair to machines and facility
  • Light fabrication of metal work and wood work
  • Painting of production equipment and facility
  • Moves production equipment in line changes
  • Changes lubrication and heat transfer fluids in production equipment
  • Maintains fans, compressors, air dryers, and chillers
  • Replaces lighting and ballasts as needed
  • Assists in companywide recycling program of materials and chemicals
  • Drives company vehicle to pick and parts and materials
  • Other maintenance related duties as assigned

Minimum Qualifications:

  • High School Diploma/GED required
  • 2 years experience in general maintenance work
  • Skills using Microsoft Word, PowerPoint and Excel
  • Must be able to drive the company vehicle
  • Valid driver’s license required
  • Immigration sponsorship not offered for this role

Physical Demands:

  • Normal working conditions absent extreme factors 
  • Must be able to see well enough to read reports, send emails, etc.
  • Must be able to hear and speak well enough to communicate with customers and fellow employees 
  • Must be able to lift up to 100lbs periodically
  • Role is deemed safety-sensitive and may be subject to employer or customer drug testing

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Skills in identifying and resolving employee, customer and vendor problems
  • Ability to communicate effectively and professionally with coworkers, customers and vendors both verbally and in writing
  • Ability to work effectively independently and as part of a team
  • Ability to follow oral and written instructions
  • Ability to organize daily activities of self and others
  • Strong desire to learn and be a problem solver
  • Ethical behavior required at all times

Position Compensation and Benefits:

  • Competitive base salaries
  • Medical
  • Dental
  • Vision
  • Employer paid STD and LTD
  • 1X your annual salary of employer paid life insurance
  • 15 days of paid vacation to start
  • 7 days of paid personal leave
  • 12 paid holidays
  • HSA Funding
  • Generous profit sharing
  • 401(k) match up to 6%

About Us

ChampionX is now part of SLB, a global technology company driving energy innovation for a balanced planet. As innovators, that’s been our mission for nearly a century.

We are a technology company that unlocks access to energy for the benefit of all. Today, we face a global imperative to create a future with more energy, but less carbon. Our diverse, innovative change makers are focused on going further in innovation and inventing the new energy technologies we need to get there.

For more news and information, visit SLB.com

Follow us on Facebook: @WeAreSLBGlobal, LinkedIn: SLBGlobal, or Instagram: @SLBGlobal

About Quartzdyne

Quartzdyne, an SLB Company, designs and manufactures precision pressure and temperature transducers for the oil and gas industry. Our proprietary offerings have become the industry standard, elevating how critical data is used to assess the performance of a reservoir and increase oil production. Our sensors are world-renowned for providing intensely accurate and high-resolution data under the most extreme conditions. At Quartzdyne, we live by our core values of Respect, Grit, Honesty, Transparency, Humility and Accountability. Quartzdyne is located along the beautiful Wasatch mountain range in Utah.

job.title: Mechanic/Millwright
job.description:

The support of an organization that believes it is vital to include and engage diverse people, perspectives, and ideas to achieve our best

  • Provides professional and timely service
  • Disassembles, cleans, inspects, and rebuilds equipment

We are looking for a Pump Repair Technician at Harbison-Fischer located in Hobbs, NM. This is your opportunity to join a global technology company, driving energy innovation for a balanced planet.

job.title: Chemical Service Technician
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

ChampionX has an immediate need for a Chemical Service Technician located in Pecos, TX.  If you are a strong operation professional with a proven track record of success working in oilfield, we invite you to apply.  This is your opportunity to join a large growing company offering a competitive base salary and benefits. 

What’s in it For You:

  • Access to best-in-class resources, tools, and technology

  • Opportunity for a long term, advanced career path

  • A culture that values safety first, including training and personal protection

  • You will join a growing company offering competitive pay and comprehensive benefits package that includes medical, dental, vision, matching 401(k) with company matching and more!

What Will You Do:

  • Provide routine service support to Sales Representatives in strategic district accounts to execute agreed upon customer system assurance plans.  

  • Assist Sales Representatives with start-ups of new applications in both existing customers and in new accounts.

  • Provide technical support to customers; identifying and resolving customer pain points, escalating as required.

  • Execute required chemistry tests, log, and report data, and maintain product inventories at assigned customer accounts.

  • Install, calibrate, and maintain chemical feed equipment and process monitoring equipment at assigned customer accounts.

  • Possible travel within assigned sales territory 10%

Minimum Qualifications:

  • High school diploma or equivalent

  • Must have a valid Driver's License and acceptable Motor Vehicle Record

  • Must be 21 years of age or older to operate a company vehicle.

  • No immigration sponsorship offered

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Bachelor's Degree or Trade School Certification

  • 2 years of successful technical service or field sales support experience

  • Must have a strong mechanical aptitude

  • Water treatment or specialty chemical industry experience

  • Working knowledge of boilers, cooling towers, and wastewater treatment systems.

  • Practical computer application literacy (including Microsoft Office Suite and ability to learn internal business systems)

  • History of working in a field that required autonomy and self-motivation

  • Prior experience that demonstrates a strong work ethic and ability to multi-task

Physical Demands:

  • Must have the ability to Lift/Push/Pull/Carry up to 55 pounds chest high.

  • Role is deemed safety-sensitive and may be subject to employer or customer drug testing.

job.title: Technical Sales Representative - Gas Lift
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

We are looking for a Technical Sales Representative -Gas Lift at PCS Ferguson located in the Texas Region. This is your opportunity to join a global technology company, driving energy innovation for a balanced planet.

What’s in it For You:

  • The ability to make an impact and shape your career with a company that is passionate about growth

  • Thrive in a company that values sustainability, drives a safety-focused culture and empowers its employees through continuous improvement

  • Comprehensive benefits package that includes medical, dental, vision, 401(k) company matching and more

What You Will Do:

  • Sell Gas Lift products and services by developing new prospects and services existing accounts.

  • Achieve sales targets and ensures customer satisfaction; research and provide forecasting for sales activities in the assigned sales territory or account.

  • Develop strong customer relationships. Identify sales opportunities that will generate future sales and repeat business through direct prospecting, following leads, and networking.

  • Accountable for delivering positive results through strong negotiations which result in significant revenue generation and profits.

  • Effectively use sales tools and processes to promote an efficient and effective sales process.

  • Effectively positions and differentiates the company from competitors; identifies competitive threats and changing market conditions.

  • Design gas lift systems, specifically to customer expectations or needs, through the use of gas lift software.

  • Deliver presentations for field or engineering teams, as well as help with troubleshooting customers wells.

  • Visit customer locations to ensure Gas Lift products and services meet with customer’s expectations.

  • Document sales activities, prepares reports, manages pipelines and perform other tasks necessary to drive sales revenue and communicate activities to sales management.

Pre-Employment Requirements:

  • Drug and alcohol test

  • Criminal background check

  • Education and certification check

  • Employment verification

  • MVR check

Minimum Qualifications:

  • Associate degree in Business, Marketing, Engineering or related field or equivalent education and/or experience.

  • 5 years of successful sales experience, within oil and gas strongly preferred Gas Lift Sales.

  • Excellent people skills, ability to build effective relationships.

  • Strong negotiation skills

  • Strong technical skills, with an understanding of artificial lift solutions.

  • Valid driver's license

  • Ability to travel up to 50%

Physical Demands:  Role is deemed safety-sensitive and may be subject to employer or customer drug testing.

  • Specific vision abilities required by this job include close vision, distance vision, peripheral vision, depth perception and ability to adjust focus.

  • The employee is regularly required to talk and hear.

  • The employee is frequently required to use hands to finger, handle, or feel and reach with hands and arms.

  • Employee is required to regularly walk in customer field locations which may include inclines, uneven surfaces, and a variety of weather conditions.

  • Employee is required to wear appropriate personal protective equipment including but not limited to shoes/boots, hard hats, or respirators.

job.title: Shop Mechanic
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

We are looking for a Shop Mechanic located in Odessa, TX, this role is responsible for installs, maintains, and repairs hydraulic jet pumps, high-pressure fluid systems. This is your opportunity to join a global technology company, driving energy innovation for a balanced planet.

What You Will Do:

  • Responsible for maintenance and repair of hydraulic systems

  • Diagnose faults in pumps, valves, motors, and hydraulic lines; replace or repair defective components

  • Test repaired or newly installed systems to ensure optimal pressure, flow, and efficiency 

  • Tear down units, disassemble pumps for inspection

  • Minor welds on bracing for piping, etc.

  • Reassemble units, prep for test

  • Knows and complies with all safety policies and procedures.

  • Contributes to the departments continuous improvement culture.

  • Others duties as required.

Minimum Qualifications:

  • High school diploma, GED, or equivalent education and/or experience

  • 2 years of similar work experience in a technical field/mechanic position

  • Welding experience

  • Experience using hand tools

  • Strong mechanical skills, preferred expertise with jet pumps

  • Strong analytical and critical thinking skills, able to identify and resolve problems.

  • Good oral and written communication skills, able to express thoughts clearly, both orally and in writing, using good grammar. 

  • Good computer skills, including Microsoft Office suite

Physical Demands:

  • The physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job.  Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.

  • The role is deemed safety-sensitive and may be subject to employer or customer drug testing.

  • The employee is regularly required to stand for the entire work day.

  • The employee regularly lifts and carries 20 lbs regularly, and 50 lbs occasionally.

  • Work is primarily executed on the production floor, with varying temperatures and high levels of noise; personal safety equipment such as glasses and protective shoes may be required.

  • Exposure to shop elements, such as, dust, petroleum-based products, and other chemicals.

  • Work may require visits to customer field locations in a variety of weather and temperature conditions.

  • May include working nights, weekends, and/or extended hours as the business need arises.

  • Specific vision abilities required by this job include close vision, distance vision, peripheral vision, depth perception and ability to adjust focus.

  • The employee is regularly required to talk or hear.

  • The employee is frequently required to use hands to finger, handle, or feel and reach with hands and arms.

  • Employee must be able to regularly bend, stoop, crawl, lay down, twist and push.

  • Employee is required to wear appropriate personal protective equipment including but not limited to shoes/boots, hard hats, or respirators.

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Oil and gas industry experience, preferred jet pumps

  • Good mechanical skills, able to perform mechanical field maintenance work to ensure optimal availability and reliability of artificial lift equipment.

  • Reliable and dependability, follows instructions, responds to management direction; Takes responsibility for own actions; Commits to long hours of work when necessary to reach goals.

  • Teamwork/Relationships, able to work with others willingly and in a spirit of cooperation and teamwork. Demonstrates a commitment to the entire organization and is supportive of all initiatives to help grow the team.

job.title: Production Operator
job.description:
ChampionX forma ahora parte de SLB, una empresa tecnológica global que impulsa la innovación energética para un planeta equilibrado. Como innovadores, esa ha sido nuestra misión durante casi un siglo. Somos una empresa tecnológica que desbloquea el acceso a la energía para el beneficio de todos. Como innovadores, esa ha sido nuestra misión durante casi un siglo. Hoy, nos enfrentamos a un imperativo global de crear un futuro con más energía, pero menos carbono. Nuestros diversos e innovadores agentes de cambio están centrados en avanzar más allá en la innovación e inventar las nuevas tecnologías energéticas que necesitamos para llegar ahí.

 

¿Qué ganas tú con esto?

 

Oportunidad para una carrera avanzada y a largo plazo en el área de Supply Chain

Acceso a recursos, herramientas y tecnología de primer nivel

Prosperar en una empresa que valora una cultura de seguridad que incluya formación de primer nivel en seguridad y equipos de protección personal

La oportunidad de impulsar cambios significativos y moldear tu carrera dentro de una empresa dinámica y orientada al crecimiento.

Apoyo de una organización comprometida con fomentar la diversidad, la inclusión y el compromiso para lograr el éxito colectivo.

Acceso a Grupos de Recursos para Empleados (ERG), facilitando la colaboración y fomentando un entorno laboral diverso e inclusivo.

Utilización de múltiples plataformas de intercambio de conocimiento para mejorar la colaboración y el compromiso entre equipos.

Disfrutar de formación remunerada que te permite aprender de profesionales exitosos.

Paquete integral de beneficios desde el primer día de empleo.

 

Lo que harás:

 

Serán responsable de la fabricación de productos mediante procesos de mezclado, utilizando fórmulas y procedimientos autorizados y siguiendo las instrucciones establecidas en las órdenes de producción, con el fin de garantizar el cumplimiento de las especificaciones y requisitos de nuestros clientes; dentro de sus principales responsabilidades se encuentran verificar la disponibilidad de materias primas y productos intermedios (cantidad, kilos y lotes), inspeccionar el correcto funcionamiento y limpieza de los equipos, reportar cualquier falla al encargado de producción, validar el peso de materias primas, ejecutar el cargue de materiales, controlar el proceso, tomar muestras de producto en proceso y producto final para análisis del laboratorio, realizar los ajustes necesarios según indicaciones, registrar la información en los formatos de control establecidos, verificar la correcta preparación de envases, rotulado, envasado y sellado, asegurar la devolución de sobrantes al almacén y la ubicación del producto terminado en el área correspondiente, así como efectuar la limpieza de los equipos y del área de trabajo al finalizar cada proceso.

 

Requisitos mínimos:

 

Técnico químico o afines.

Conocimiento y manejo básico  de las herramientas de office (Excel, PowerPoint, Word).

1 años de experiencia en cargos afines Conocimientos en procesos industriales

 

Exigencias físicas:

El puesto se considera sensible a la seguridad y puede estar sujeto a pruebas de drogas por parte del empleador o del cliente

 

Acerca de ChampionX:

 

ChampionX es un líder mundial en la provisión de programas y servicios de química, tecnología de perforación, soluciones de elevación artificial y tecnologías de automatización para la industria del petróleo y gas aguas arriba y Midstream. Nuestro compromiso con la diversidad y la inclusión

Estamos comprometidos con un trato justo e igualitario para empleados y candidatos. Reclutamos, contratamos, promocionamos, transferimos y ofrecemos oportunidades de ascenso basadas en las cualificaciones individuales y el desempeño laboral. En todos los asuntos que afecten al empleo, la compensación, los beneficios, las condiciones laborales y las oportunidades de ascenso, no discriminaremos a ningún empleado o solicitante de empleo por motivos de raza, religión, color, credo, origen nacional, estatus de ciudadanía, sexo, orientación sexual, identidad y expresión de género, información genética, estado civil, edad, discapacidad o condición de veterano cubierto.

ChampionX ahora forma parte de SLB, una empresa tecnológica global que impulsa la innovación energética para lograr un planeta equilibrado. Como innovadores, es nuestra misión desde hace casi un siglo.

 

Somos una empresa tecnológica que facilita el acceso a la energía para el beneficio de todos. Como innovadores, esa ha sido nuestra misión durante casi un siglo. Hoy, nos enfrentamos al imperativo global de crear un futuro con más energía, pero con menos carbono. Nuestros diversos e innovadores creadores de cambios se centran en profundizar en la innovación e inventar las nuevas tecnologías energéticas que necesitamos para lograrlo.

We believe the best teams are diverse and inclusive, and we are on a journey to create a workplace where every employee can grow and achieve their best. job.title: Quality Leader Control
job.description:
ChampionX forma ahora parte de SLB, una empresa tecnológica global que impulsa la innovación energética para un planeta equilibrado. Como innovadores, esa ha sido nuestra misión durante casi un siglo. Somos una empresa tecnológica que desbloquea el acceso a la energía para el beneficio de todos. Como innovadores, esa ha sido nuestra misión durante casi un siglo. Hoy, nos enfrentamos a un imperativo global de crear un futuro con más energía, pero menos carbono. Nuestros diversos e innovadores agentes de cambio están centrados en avanzar más allá en la innovación e inventar las nuevas tecnologías energéticas que necesitamos para llegar ahí.

 

¿Qué ganas tú con esto?

 

Oportunidad para una carrera avanzada y a largo plazo en el área de Supply Chain

Acceso a recursos, herramientas y tecnología de primer nivel

Prosperar en una empresa que valora una cultura de seguridad que incluya formación de primer nivel en seguridad y equipos de protección personal

La oportunidad de impulsar cambios significativos y moldear tu carrera dentro de una empresa dinámica y orientada al crecimiento.

Apoyo de una organización comprometida con fomentar la diversidad, la inclusión y el compromiso para lograr el éxito colectivo.

Acceso a Grupos de Recursos para Empleados (ERG), facilitando la colaboración y fomentando un entorno laboral diverso e inclusivo.

Utilización de múltiples plataformas de intercambio de conocimiento para mejorar la colaboración y el compromiso entre equipos.

Disfrutar de formación remunerada que te permite aprender de profesionales exitosos.

Paquete integral de beneficios desde el primer día de empleo.

 

Lo que harás:

 

Seras el responsable de liderar, coordinar y asegurar el adecuado funcionamiento de las operaciones de control de calidad, garantizando la calidad, precisión y confiabilidad de los análisis químicos, físico–químicos y operacionales. Su rol abarca la supervisión del equipo técnico, la gestión de recursos, el mantenimiento de equipos, el cumplimiento normativo y la implementación de prácticas seguras que aseguren operaciones eficientes y alineadas con los estándares corporativos y regulatorios. Asimismo, participa activamente en la planificación, estandarización de procedimientos, control de calidad, interpretación de resultados y soporte técnico a áreas operativas o clientes internos.

Responsable de la supervisión de las actividades y procesos del laboratorio, para garantizar en tiempo, calidad y eficiencia el aseguramiento de la calidad de las Materias Primas y Productos fabricados en la Planta.

Da soporte técnico al equipo de ventas, da recomendaciones para el mantenimiento de equipos usados en campo.

Gestion de la compra de insumos, reactivos y equipos para laboratorio.

Coordina con el área de Procurement la aprobación de proveedores alternos y tiene la autoridad para rechazarlos, según sus criterios técnicos y experiencia.

Prepara procedimientos para la reutilización, recuperación o eliminación del material no conforme, así como acciones correctivas, teniendo la autoridad para la disposición final de los materiales.

Responsable de la correcta gestión visual en el laboratorio de los equipos de inspección, medición y prueba, aplicando programas de mantenimiento y calibración. Tiene la autoridad para hacer los cambios requeridos.

Desarrollar proyectos a través de análisis físico-químico en campo.

Controla la documentación y registro de su área de acuerdo con los procedimientos establecidos, de la introducción de nuevos productos en el sistema y del mantenimiento del mismo teniendo la autoridad de hacer los cambios requeridos.

Responsable de la detección, seguimiento y corrección de los productos no conformes, así como de la aplicación efectiva de las acciones correctivas necesarias para evitar que las no conformidades se repitan.

Brinda soporte técnico a los laboratorios de los clientes de ChampionX de Ecuador  a través de reportes y visitas.

Soportar y está comprometido con el proceso de mejoramiento de la calidad de ChampionX de Ecuador

Responsable del cumplimiento de la estrategia de TPM y Lean Manufacturing con su equipo de trabajo.

 

Requisitos mínimos:

 

Profesional en Química o Químico Farmacéutico o afines

Conocimiento y manejo intermedio de las herramientas de office (Excel, PowerPoint, Word).

8 años de experiencia en laboratorio químico.

Manejo avanzado del Idioma Inglés (fluidez oral y escrita).

Experiencia en manejo de equipos de instrumentación analítica y calibraciones.

Experiencia en uso de software SAP o similares.

Conocimiento en control estadístico de procesos.

 

Exigencias físicas:

El puesto se considera sensible a la seguridad y puede estar sujeto a pruebas de drogas por parte del empleador o del cliente

 

Acerca de ChampionX:

 

ChampionX es un líder mundial en la provisión de programas y servicios de química, tecnología de perforación, soluciones de elevación artificial y tecnologías de automatización para la industria del petróleo y gas aguas arriba y Midstream.
