job.title: Plunger Service Representative
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

We are looking for a Plunger Lift Service Representative for our PCSF business unit, located in Vernal, UT. This is your opportunity to join a global technology company, driving energy innovation for a balanced planet.

What’s in it For You:

  • The ability to make an impact and shape your career with a company that is passionate about growth.

  • Thrive in a company that values sustainability, drives a safety-focused culture and empowers through continuous improvement.

  • The chance to join a learning and problem-solving culture

  • Comprehensive benefits package that includes medical, dental, vision, 401(k) with company matching and more!

What You Will Do:

  • Reviews quotes, installation and repair plans to ensure a thorough understanding of customer needs and the parts and process required to address the need.

  • Explains installation, service or repair plans, and invoices to customers; discusses cost or time overruns with team leader or manager.

  • Delivers and installs plunger artificial lift equipment for the customer.

  • Executes installations, repair, or service plans and documents results.

  • Creates product failure analysis reports.

  • Develops good working relationships with customers through professional communication.

  • Ships, receives and transfers parts and equipment for the warehouse.

  • Maintains accurate inventory levels and communicates any discrepancies as needed.

  • Knows and complies with all safety policies and procedures.

  • Other duties as assigned

Minimum Requirements:

  • High school diploma or equivalent  

  • Valid driver's license in state of residence required

  • Must be 21 years of age to operate company vehicle.

  • 4 years of relevant experience in the oil and gas industry.

  • Strong mechanic skills

  • Ability to troubleshoot customers issues

  • Good verbal and written communication skills

  • Strong safety mindset

Pre-Employment Requirements:

  • Motor vehicle record check

  • Drug and alcohol test

  • Criminal background check

  • Education and certification check

Physical Demands:

  • Must be able to lift and carry 50 lbs regularly.

  • Specific vision abilities required by this job include close vision, distance vision, peripheral vision, depth perception and ability to adjust focus.

  • The employee is regularly required to talk or hear.

  • The employee is frequently required to use hands to finger, handle, or feel and reach with hands and arms.

  • Employee is required to occasionally walk in customer field locations which may include inclines, uneven surfaces, and a variety of weather conditions.

  • Employee must be able to regularly bend, stoop, crawl, lay down, twist and push.

  • Employee is required to wear appropriate personal protective equipment including but not limited to shoes/boots, hard hats, or respirators.

About Us:

ChampionX is now part of SLB, a global technology company driving energy innovation for a balanced planet. As innovators, that’s been our mission for nearly a century.

We are a technology company that unlocks access to energy for the benefit of all. As innovators, that’s been our mission for nearly a century. Today, we face a global imperative to create a future with more energy, but less carbon. Our diverse, innovative change makers are focused on going further in innovation and inventing the new energy technologies we need to get there.

For more news and information, visit SLB.com

Follow us on Facebook at @WeAreSLBGlobal, LinkedIn: SLBGlobal, or Instagram at @SLBGlobal.

Our Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion 

We believe the best teams are diverse and inclusive, and we are on a journey to create a workplace where every employee can grow and achieve their best. We are committed to fair and equal treatment of employees and applicants. We recruit, hire, promote, transfer and provide opportunities for advancement on the basis of individual qualifications and job performance. In all matters affecting employment, compensation, benefits, working conditions, and opportunities for advancement, we will not discriminate against any employee or applicant for employment because of race, religion, color, creed, national origin, citizenship status, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity and expressions, genetic information, marital status, age, disability, or status as a covered veteran.

job.title: Rod Painter
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration. We are looking for a Rod Painter located in Tulsa, OK. This is your opportunity to join a global technology company, driving energy innovation for a balanced planet.

What’s in it For You:

  • The support of an organization that believes it is vital to include and engage diverse people, perspectives, and ideas to achieve our best
  • Thrive in a company that values sustainability, drives a safety-focused culture, and empowers through continuous improvement

What You Will Do:

  • Operates painting equipment and auxiliary equipment.
  • Tests and mixes paint as required. 
  • Keeps paint pots supplied with paint of correct quality.
  • Starts up and shuts down the equipment as required.
  • Observes rods on conveyors as they pass through the unit, culling out crooked rods.
  • Keeps rods separated and packaged for each separate size and grade. Paints identification color on protectors.
  • Responsible for quality and quantity of work helpers.
  • Obtains reasonable production with minimum supervision.
  • Maintains work area and equipment in a clean and orderly manner.
  • Informs supervisor of any malfunction of equipment.

Minimum Qualifications:

  • Two year technical degree or equivalent education and/or experience. Valid driver's license in state of residence required.

Physical Demands:  

  • Must be able to lift and carry 50 lbs regularly, and 100 lbs occasionally.
  • Specific vision abilities required by this job include close vision, distance vision, peripheral vision, depth perception and ability to adjust focus.
  • The employee is regularly required to talk or hear.
  • The employee is frequently required to use hands to finger, handle, or feel and reach with hands and arms.
  • Employee is required to occasionally walk in customer field locations which may include inclines, uneven surfaces, and a variety of weather conditions.
  • Employee must be able to regularly bend, stoop, crawl, lay down, twist and push.
  • Employee is required to wear appropriate personal protective equipment including but not limited to  shoes/boots, hard hats, or respirators.

job.title: Sr. Chemist
job.description:
Job Description

Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

This Senior Chemist position focuses on applying advanced chemical expertise to design and execute laboratory testing, analyze complex data, and support research and development activities. The role requires strong leadership, including opportunities to mentor junior chemists, guiding technical workstreams, and driving best practices in safety, quality, and efficiency, while managing a high-volume workload of up to 1,000 samples per month. Working closely with a small, collaborative team of two other chemists and reporting directly to the Regional Lab Manager, this individual must prioritize teamwork and effective communication to ensure project success. The ideal candidate brings 5+ years of laboratory experience, proven leadership ability, and strong problem-solving skills within a highly team-oriented environment.

The successful candidate will be a technically strong analytical chemist who thrives in a small-team environment, is comfortable managing a high-volume analytical workload, takes ownership of responsibilities, communicates effectively with customers and colleagues, and contributes positively to a collaborative and accountable laboratory culture.

Minimum Education & Experience Requirements

  • Bachelor's degree in Chemistry or related field with at least 5 years of US Land oilfield production chemistry experience, or
  • Master's degree in Chemistry or related field with at least 3 years of US Land oilfield production chemistry experience
  • Demonstrated experience supporting production chemistry programs for U.S. land-based oil and gas operations.
  • Experience managing high volumes of routine analytical testing while maintaining quality, accuracy, and turnaround time expectations.
  • Proven ability to follow SOPs, work independently, prioritize competing demands, and manage moderately complex projects with minimal supervision.
  • Experience operating and troubleshooting ICP-OES, Ion Chromatography (IC), Gas Chromatography (GC), XRF, or equivalent analytical instrumentation.
  • Proficient in Microsoft Office Suite (Outlook, Excel, PowerPoint, etc) and Laboratory Information Management Systems (LIMS), with experience leveraging data management and reporting tools to support laboratory operations, decision-making, and process improvement.

Required Skills & Experience (Must – Haves)

  • Demonstrated success working within a small laboratory team where collaboration, flexibility, and individual accountability are critical to overall laboratory performance.
  • Demonstrate initiative and ability to work independently and handle multiple tasks.
  • Demonstrated ability to adapt to changing priorities while maintaining a strong commitment to safety, quality, and customer responsiveness.
  • Demonstrated desire and initiative to grow into leadership roles over time, including taking on increased responsibility, mentoring peers, and contributing to team development.
  • Strong analytical, organizational, and problem-solving skills with exceptional attention to detail.
  • Effective verbal and written communication skills with the ability to communicate technical information to both technical and non-technical audiences.
  • Physically able to lift 50 pounds, climb stairs or ladder, and agile enough to squat and bend over.
  • Ability to independently prioritize tasks, manage multiple projects, and meet deadlines with minimal supervision.
  • Strong organizational skills and ability to manage a high-volume workload (handling up to 1,000 samples per month).

Preferred Skills & Experience (Nice to Have)

  • 5–10 years of experience in oilfield production chemistry, chemical manufacturing, or industrial analytical laboratory environments.
  •  3+ years of laboratory leadership, technical leadership, mentoring, or senior laboratory experience supporting production chemistry operations.
  •  Demonstrated history of taking ownership of laboratory responsibilities, following through on commitments, and consistently delivering high-quality results.
  • Proven ability to foster a collaborative, respectful, and solutions-oriented work environment through effective communication and constructive engagement with colleagues and stakeholders.
  • Experience presenting technical findings, managing projects, and collaborating effectively across functional teams.
  • Understanding of customer needs and ability to translate analytical data into actionable operational insights.
  • Experience identifying and implementing process improvements that enhance laboratory efficiency, quality, safety, or customer service. 

Job Duties:

  • Lead and execute routine and non-routine analytical testing activities in support of customer programs, field operations, and laboratory service commitments.
  • Apply advanced production chemistry knowledge and sound technical judgment to troubleshoot challenges, validate methods, interpret analytical data, and solve complex problems.
  • Support customer programs by designing, conducting, and interpreting laboratory analyses tied to real-world field performance and operational outcomes.
  • Independently manage multiple priorities, project deadlines, customer requests, and stakeholder communications in a fast-paced laboratory environment.
  • Manage high volumes of routine analytical testing while maintaining quality, accuracy, data integrity, and turnaround time expectations.
  • Operate, maintain, troubleshoot, and optimize laboratory instrumentation while ensuring compliance with established QA/QC procedures and laboratory standards.
  • Support the mentoring, training, and development of chemists, technicians, and technologists through coaching, knowledge sharing, and technical guidance.
  • Collaborate with field personnel, sales, technical teams, and customers to translate analytical data into actionable recommendations.
  • Identify opportunities for process improvements that enhance laboratory efficiency, quality, safety, and customer service.
  • Champion safety culture, support continuous improvement initiatives, and contribute positively to overall laboratory performance, efficiency, and team effectiveness.

Perform miscellaneous administrative tasks.

ChampionX is now part of SLB, a global technology company driving energy innovation for a

balanced planet. As innovators, that’s been our mission for nearly a century.

job.title: Logistics Associate
job.description:
We are recruiting for a Logistics Operator located in Georgetown. This is your opportunity to join a growing company offering a competitive base salary and benefits.

What’s in it For You

  • The ability to make an impact and shape your career at a company that is passionate about growth.

  • The support of an organization that believes it is vital to include and engage diverse people, perspectives, and ideas to achieve our best.

  • Thrive in a company that values sustainability, drives a safety-oriented culture and empowers through continuous improvement.

  • Comprehensive benefits package.

What You Will Do

  • Maintaining warehouse facility to ensure proper handlings of chemicals, namely, receiving & dispatching.

  • Operate batch processing equipment which include tanks, pumps, charging systems, bulk loading and packaging systems and forklifts.

  • Ensure incoming materials are stored, stacked, and distributed according to company procedures and standard operating procedures (SOP).

  • Manage stock levels by participating in stock taking and cycle counting. Take inventory of raw and intermediate materials for ordering purposes.

  • Prepare finished goods for packing and dispatch.

  • Produce the highest standard of work possible in accordance with the company’s procedures.

  • Prepare drums and tote tanks for loading and shipping which includes labelling in accordance with company and government regulations.

  • Operate all pumps for shipping and receiving of bulk materials and cleaning tote tank when needed.

  • Operating of company forklifts & vehicles in day-to-day operations.

  • Blending of chemicals according to company blend sheets.

  • Perform all required site inspections & housekeeping.

  • Follow and promote continuous improvement of performance, safety and environmental policies and procedures.

  • Practice good housekeeping in all areas of the facility.

  • Carry out any other function necessary for the smooth running of warehouse function.

Basic Requirements

  • Minimum high school graduate or CSEC

  • Supply Chain Certificate will be a plus

  • 2 years of logistics related experience would be an asset.

  • Proficient in operating forklifts, with forklift license.

  • Knowledge of the structure and content of the English language

  • Knowledge and understanding of plant and warehouse operations and ability to maintain quality and safety standards.

  • Experience with SAP will be a plus

Basic Competencies

  • Ability to foster effective working relationships within a team environment.

  • Organizing and coordinating skills and ability to communicate effectively, both orally and in writing.

  • Problem-solving skills with an analytical mind

Physical Demands

  • Must be able to lift and carry a minimum of 50 lbs.

job.title: Production Planner
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. This position will be hired directly with SLB.

We are looking for a Production Planner located in Nisku, AB. This is your opportunity to join a global technology company, driving energy innovation for a balanced planet.

What’s In It for You:

The support of an organization that believes it is vital to include and engage diverse people, perspectives, and ideas to achieve our best

  • Thrive in a company that values sustainability, drives a safety-focused culture, and empowers through continuous improvement
  • Comprehensive benefits package that includes extended health and dental benefits, a company-matched retirement program, and more!

What You Will Do:

  • Plan maintenance activities for the facility.
  • Plan and confirm priorities with the Technology Lifecycle Management (TLM) Maintenance Manager, Maintenance Supervisor and Asset Coordinator.
  • Create and schedule work orders for the assembly, testing and repair of Electrical Submersible Pumps.
  • Open repair work orders and enter information to document equipment scheduled for service.
  • Organize equipment in the appropriate queues depending on priorities and needs.
  • Coordinate with Stores on assigning and delivering materials and supplies to workstations, ensuring work is completed according to maintenance and repair priorities.
  • Supervise Computerized Maintenance Management System (CMMS) Clerks where such personnel are employed.  
  • Assist the TLM Maintenance Manager and Maintenance Supervisor with asset counting and equipment list management functions.
  • Contribute to the Health, Safety and Environment, continuous improvement and LEAN programs.

Pre-Employment Requirements:

  • Drug and alcohol testing
  • Fitness-to-work testing
  • Background check

Position Details:

  • Working at the Nisku manufacturing facility
  • Full-time
  • Permanent
  • Monday to Friday, 8:00 am to 4:30 pm

Minimum Qualifications:

  • High school diploma
  • Proficient in Microsoft Office
  • Good verbal and written communication skills

Preferred Qualifications:

  • SAP Experience
  • Administrative or scheduling experience

job.title: Account Manager
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

We are looking for an Account Manager located in Bonnyville, AB, area working a regular 5 day – 40 hour work week.  The Account Manager will be primarily responsible for servicing existing programs as well as developing and applying chemical solutions for remediation of customer challenges within targeted accounts in the sales area.

You will be a self starter that has excellent time management skills and a desire to meet customer demands. Strong communication skills are a requirement for this role. In addition, you will be responsible for building long-term relationships throughout your customer base by understanding their key business drivers, executing system assurance programs, and deploying new technologies.

If you have experience as an oilfield operator, as an engineer/engineering technologist or have a proven track record of success in technical sales, we invite you to apply. This is your opportunity to join a large growing company offering a competitive base salary, bonus structure and benefits.

What’s in it For You:

  • Flexible, independent work environment where you will manage a monthly schedule

  • Access to best-in-class resources, tools, and technology

  • Grow your income as you drive growth

  • Thrive in a company that values a culture of safety to include top-notch safety training and personal protection equipment

  • Comprehensive benefits package that includes extended health benefits, a company-paid pension plan, and more!

What You Will Do:

  • Work closely with current and new customers within an assigned territory/customer base.  Understand customer business needs and recommend continuous improvement and innovation plans that will drive customer loyalty.

  • Develop strong relationships with key stakeholders in current and new customer organizations, including plant or facility executives.

  • Generate and execute sales plans in assigned customer base and new customer accounts to meet defined territory profit increase goals.

  • Provide technical support to customers; identifying and resolving customer issues, escalating as required.

  • Engage in problem solving by performing system analysis, interpreting data and providing written recommendations to ensure customer operations are performing at optimal levels.

  • Possible travel within assigned sales territory

Position Details:

  • Candidate must reside within the Lakeland/Bonnyville region.

  • No immigration sponsorship offered.

  • This is a safety sensitive position, and the successful candidate will be required to complete drug and alcohol testing.

  • Must have a valid Driver’s License and acceptable Motor Vehicle Record

Minimum Qualifications:

  • 3 -5 years of technical sales, operating/engineering experience is preferred but not required.

  • High computer application literacy (including Microsoft Office Suite, and ability to learn internal business systems)

  • Prior experience that demonstrates a strong work ethic and ability to multitask

Physical Demands:

  • Must have the ability to Lift/Push/Pull/Carry up to 55 pounds chest high.

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Experience in the oil and gas industry preferred

  • Previous Petrochemical experience

  • Water treatment or specialty chemical industry experience

  • Working knowledge of water treatment systems

  • History of working in a field that required autonomy and self-motivation

  • Prior experience that required excellent communication skills

  • Prior experience that required excellent organizational skills

  • Account leadership experience that includes calling on multilevel plant or facilities management to accomplish company goals

job.title: Lead Chemist - EOR
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

We are looking for a Lead Chemist for our New Product Development: Nanotechnology team, located in Sugar Land, TX. This role will support with innovation projects leveraging nanotechnology for use in production chemistry.

The ideal candidate is highly versatile, communicates effectively, and thrives in a collaborative environment. If you are a passionate professional that likes to work in an autonomous, hands-on environment, we invite you to apply. This is your opportunity to join a growing company offering a competitive base salary and benefits.

What’s in it For You:

  • The ability to make an impact and shape your career with a company that is passionate about growth 

  • The support of an organization that believes it is vital to include and engage diverse people, perspectives, and ideas to achieve our best 

  • The opportunity to thrive in a company that values sustainability, drives a safety-focused culture and empowers its employees through continuous improvement 

  • A comprehensive benefits package starting day 1 of employment 

What You Will Do:

  • Perform work in a safe manner while identifying opportunities for improving safety.

  • Act as key technical contributor on projects, experiments and research of moderate scope and complexity with high impact on the business.

  • Act with considerate latitude in determining technical approach required to meet desired objectives.

  • Demonstrate full use and application of scientific, project management, design excellence, and other principles, theories and concepts to assignments where precedents may exist plus working knowledge of other related disciplines.

  • Provide solutions to a wide range of difficult problems such as the design of complex systems and programs and/or the resolution of problems requiring state of the art awareness of the terminology.

  • Demonstrate initiative and creativity in identifying and trying new and different approaches.

  • Show understanding of our customers (internal and external) products and available technologies to effectively identify creative solutions that meet customer requirements. 

  • Demonstrate the ability to translate understanding of customer challenges into new solutions or product ideas.

  • May supervise others or serve as a technical resource and mentor in area of expertise.  Acts as a project or technical lead for strategic projects.

  • Demonstrates continuous improvement mindset and ability to modify processes to improve internal efficiency.

  • Manage time effectively and prioritizes assigned tasks to ensure delivery.

Minimum Qualifications:

  • Degree in chemistry, chemical engineering, or related technical field, preferably with a background in EOR techniques and application.

  • PhD and 0 - 2 years of experience, MS and 3 years minimum experience, BS and 5 years minimum experience.

  • Excellent oral and written communication skills. 

  • Demonstrated project management and organizational skills.

  • Immigration VISA sponsorship will be evaluated on a case-by-case basis

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Problem solver with ability to lead change, manage ambiguity, and be able to adapt to new situations and projects quickly.

  • Experience with Enhanced Oil Recovery (EOR) based applications, and methodology associated with testing the same such as corefloods.

  • Experience with nanoparticles and additives used in EOR applications.

  • Experience with organic synthesis of small molecules.

  • Formulation experience developing new products, and improving existing products.

Physical Demands:

  • Role is deemed safety-sensitive and may be subject to employer or customer drug testing

job.title: CEOR Group Leader
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

We have an immediate opening for an RD&E Group Leader for our Chemically Enhanced Oil Recovery (CEOR) Technology team in Sugar Land, TX. This individual will be responsible for leading the development and deployment of innovative technology solutions to achieve Recovery related business objectives. The CEOR RD&E Group Leader will report to the CEOR Technology Manager for the PCT Technology team and work in close collaboration with global business, Technology, New Product Development (NPD), Marketing, and Sales counterparts ensure inter-team and global connectivity.

What’s in it For You:

  • You will join a growing company offering competitive pay and benefits

  • Access to best-in-class resources, tools, and technology

  • Opportunity for a long term, advanced career path

  • A culture that values safety first, including training and personal protection

What You Will Do:

  • Lead the CEOR Technology team by ensuring all team members perform their duties safely, efficiently, and in alignment with quality standards, while fostering innovation and continuous technical and personal/professional growth.

  • Lead and coordinate Technology resources both on direct team in Sugar Land, TX and globally in other locations in accordance with business strategy, needs, and impact.

  • Develops organizational talent and succession plans and provides ongoing coaching and mentoring for staff. Owns the strategy for team and organizational development, including safety and employee engagement.

  • Ensures ongoing external awareness of technology, competitors, and emerging and disruptive opportunities.

  • Engage directly with requestors to clarify needs, define project scope, and ensure alignment of expectations.

  • Maintain clear and consistent communication with team members to support effective and efficient daily operations.

  • Recruit, onboard, and train new team members as needed to support team growth and capability development.

  • Promote a culture of safety by modeling safe work practices and identifying opportunities for continuous safety improvement.

  • Manage multiple projects in a dynamic, fast-paced environment, ensuring timely and high-quality deliverables.

  • Actively participate in the broader RD&E community to contribute to business growth and cross-functional collaboration.

Minimum Qualifications:

  • Ph.D. in Materials Science, Chemistry, Chemical Engineering or related field with 4 years of relevant work experience or M.S. with 6 years of relevant work experience or B.S. with 10 years of relevant work experience.

  • Track record of successful technology development, new product / service launch and leveraging of external technology

  • Proven ability to motivate and lead individuals as part of a high-performing team.

  • Strong commitment to safety, including safe handling of chemicals and equipment, and promoting a safe work environment for colleagues and self.

  • Excellent time management skills and organizational skills.

  • Excellent written, oral, and visual communication skills to communicate effectively with all levels of the organization, both within and outside the business.

  • Proficient knowledge of MS Word, Excel, PowerPoint, and Outlook.

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Previous supervisory experience.

  • Experience with enhanced oil recovery methods/business.

  • Experience with Specialty Chemicals in the Oil & Gas industry.

Physical Demands:

  • Role is deemed safety-sensitive and may be subject to employer or customer drug testing

  • Must be able to lift up to 30 pounds

job.title: Application Specialist
job.description:
Job Description

Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

If you are a service professional diligent in time management, we invite you to apply. 

This is your opportunity to join a large growing company offering competitive pay and benefits (hourly pay).  You will be primarily responsible for working with Sales Representatives on executing service plans in strategic district accounts.   You will be required to assist District Sales Representatives with start-ups of both new applications in current customers, and in transitioning competitively held accounts to ChampionX. In addition, you must install, maintain, and calibrate chemical feed equipment and process monitoring equipment at multiple customer locations.

What’s in it For You:

  • You will join a growing company offering competitive pay and comprehensive benefits package that includes medical, dental, vision, matching 401(k) with company matching and more!

  • Access to best-in-class resources, tools, and technology

  • Opportunity for a long term, advanced career path

  • A culture that values safety first, including training and personal protection

What You Will Do:

  • Provide routine service support to Sales Representatives in strategic district accounts to execute agreed upon customer system assurance plans.  

  • Assist Sales Representatives with start-ups of new applications in both existing customers and in new accounts.

  • Provide technical support to customers; identifying and resolving customer pain points, escalating as required.

  • Execute required chemistry tests, log, and report data, and maintain product inventories at assigned customer accounts.

  • Install, calibrate, and maintain chemical feed equipment and process monitoring equipment at assigned customer accounts.

  • Possible travel within assigned sales territory 25%

Position Details:

  • 25% overnight travel required to support assigned customer accounts

Minimum Qualifications:

  • High School Diploma or equivalent

  • Must have a valid Driver's License and acceptable Motor Vehicle Record

  • Must be 21 years of age or older to operate company vehicle

  • Immigration sponsorship not offered for this role

Physical Demands:

  • Must have the ability to Lift/Push/Pull/Carry up to 55 pounds chest high.

  • Role is deemed safety-sensitive and may be subject to employer or customer drug testing.

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Associate’s Degree or Trade School Certification

  • 2 years of successful technical service or field sales support experience

  • Must have a strong mechanical aptitude

  • Water treatment or specialty chemical industry experience

  • Working knowledge of boilers, cooling towers, and wastewater treatment systems.

  • Practical computer application literacy (including Microsoft Office Suite and ability to learn internal business systems)

  • History of working in a field that required autonomy and self-motivation

  • Prior experience that demonstrates a strong work ethic and ability to multi-task

job.title: Engineering Manager
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

We are looking for a Engineering Manager located in Rockyview, AB (near Calgary).

The Engineering Manager supports the execution of engineered‑to‑order fabrication projects by bridging engineering, sales, operations, supply chain, and project management.

This role is focused on front‑end engineering, technical evaluation, and feasibility assessment during the inquiry and quoting phase, while also supporting early project setup for awarded work.

The Engineering Manager plays a key role in ensuring that customer requirements, technical specifications, material availability, and shop capacity are aligned before commercial commitments are finalized, reducing execution risk and improving project predictability.

This position is intended as a developmental role that provides broad exposure to the full project lifecycle and prepares the individual for deeper technical, project, or operational leadership responsibilities over time.

What’s in it For You:

  • A flexible and independent work environment that fosters a good work-life balance.

  • Access to best-in-class resources, tools, and technology.

  • Thrive in a company that values sustainability, drives a safety-focused culture and empowers its employees through continuous improvement.

  • Comprehensive benefits package that includes extended health benefits, vision, dental, prescription drugs, travel insurance, life insurance, and more!

  • Company-paid pension plan, employee and family assistance program, paid vacation, sick and personal days.

What You Will Do:

Front‑End Engineering & Quoting Support

  • Review customer specifications, datasheets, and inquiry documentation to establish technical requirements and design basis

  • Support conceptual and preliminary design development in collaboration with Engineering

  • Prepare technical input for quotes, including:

    • Scope definition

    • Design assumptions

    • Key risks and exclusions

  • Identify technical complexity, fabrication challenges, and execution constraints early in the inquiry phase

  • Support engineering‑led pricing by providing design‑based input into material and labor assumptions

Cross‑Functional Feasibility & Risk Assessment

  • Coordinate internally with:

    • Engineering to confirm design feasibility and standards

    • Operations to validate high‑level shop loading, sequencing, and execution constraints

    • Supply Chain to identify long‑lead materials, vendor risks, and procurement constraints

  • Surface execution risks, constraints, and trade‑off options prior to finalizing delivery commitments

  • Document key assumptions and risks to enable informed commercial decision‑making

Project Handover & Early Execution Support

  • Support clean handoff of awarded projects to Project Management, including:

    • Defined scope and assumptions

    • Identified risks and mitigation strategies

    • Technical clarification of quoted configurations

  • Assist with early engineering coordination to ensure drawings, documentation, and material requirements align with execution strategy

  • Provide technical clarification during early project phases as required

Technical Communication & Continuous Improvement

  • Act as a technical point of contact for internal clarification questions during quoting and early project phases

  • Support development and refinement of standard designs, templates, and estimating inputs

  • Capture lessons learned from executed projects to improve future feasibility assessments and quoting accuracy

  • Improve clarity and consistency of internal communication between functions

Reporting Relationship

  • Reports functionally within Engineering.

  • Works closely with Sales, Project Management, Operations, and Supply Chain

  • Receives direction and prioritization in support of commercial commitments from the Site / Business Manager

Position Details:

  • Candidate must reside in Calgary, AB or the surrounding area.

  • Work schedule: 4 days from the office and 1 day from home.

  • Full-time position, 40 hours/week.

  • Immigration sponsorship is not available for this position.

Minimum Qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s degree in engineering (Mechanical, Chemical, Industrial, or related discipline).

  • P.Eng required.

  • 5+ years of experience in an engineered‑to‑order, fabrication, or industrial manufacturing environment.

  • Any software or program requirements?

  • Strong technical aptitude with the ability to interpret specifications and drawings

  • Working knowledge of fabrication processes, materials, and industrial equipment

  • Strong analytical and problem‑solving skills

  • Ability to communicate technical information clearly across disciplines

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Experience supporting proposals, estimating, or front‑end engineering

  • Exposure to shop operations, production planning, or supply chain coordination

  • Familiarity with pressure equipment, mechanical design, or industrial process systems

  • Experience in cross‑functional or matrixed environments

Key Competencies & Attributes

  • Systems‑oriented thinker with strong technical judgment

  • Comfortable working with incomplete information and managing ambiguity

  • Able to challenge assumptions constructively and professionally

  • Organized, detail‑oriented, and disciplined in documentation

  • Respected communicator who can work effectively across functions

  • Interest in developing broad business and execution understanding, beyond pure design work

About Us

ChampionX is now part of SLB, a global technology company driving energy innovation for a balanced planet. As innovators, that’s been our mission for nearly a century.

We are a technology company that unlocks access to energy for the benefit of all. As innovators, that’s been our mission for nearly a century. Today, we face a global imperative to create a future with more energy, but less carbon. Our diverse, innovative change makers are focused on going further in innovation and inventing the new energy technologies we need to get there.

For more news and information, visit SLB.com

Follow us on Facebook at @WeAreSLBGlobal, LinkedIn: SLBGlobal, or Instagram at @SLBGlobal.

job.title: Principal Chemist
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

We are looking for a Principal Chemist for our Production Chemicals Technology team, located in Sugar Land, TX. This role will drive the chemistry and product sourcing efforts in support of the Production Systems, Well Construction and Reservoir Performance businesses. 

The ideal candidate is highly versatile, communicates effectively, and thrives in a collaborative environment. If you are a passionate professional that likes to work in an autonomous, hands-on environment, we invite you to apply. This is your opportunity to join a growing company offering a competitive base salary and benefits.

What’s in it For You:

  • The ability to make an impact and shape your career with a company that is passionate about growth

  • The support of an organization that believes it is vital to include and engage diverse people, perspectives, and ideas to achieve our best

  • Thrive in a company that values sustainability, and drives a safety-focused culture

  • Comprehensive benefits package starting day 1 of employment- medical, dental, vision, matching 401k, and more!

What You Will Do:

  • Perform work in a safe manner while identifying opportunities for improving safety.

  • Execute on projects, experiments and research of critical chemical components/products with high complexity along with high impact on the business.

  • Collaborate with multiple SLB divisions and business stakeholders/functions to determine best synthetic routes for cost effective scale up and deployment.

  • Collaborate with cross-division technical groups to qualify materials for their respective applications.

  • Demonstrate full use and application of scientific, project management, design excellence, and other principles, theories and concepts to assignments where precedents may exist plus working knowledge of other related disciplines.

  • Provide solutions to a wide range of chemistry challenges associated with various drilling and completion applications via structure property relationships and foundational chemistry/engineering approaches.

  • Demonstrate initiative and creativity in identifying and trying new and different approaches.

  • Demonstrate the ability to translate understanding of chemistry/product compositions into viable products that are able to be manufactured internally.

  • Demonstrate continuous improvement mindset and ability to modify processes to improve internal efficiency.

  • Manage time effectively and prioritize assigned tasks to ensure delivery.

Minimum Qualifications:

  • Degree in chemistry, chemical engineering, or related technical field.

  • BS in Chemistry or Chemical Engineering or related field with six years of relevant work experience or MS with four years of relevant work experience or PhD with fewer than two years of work experience

  • Experience in organic chemistry with the ability to synthesize a wide range of chemistries, including small molecules, surfactants, polymers and other related materials.

  • Problem solver with ability to lead change, manage ambiguity, and be able to adapt to new situations and projects quickly.

  • Excellent oral and written communication skills.  Ability to connect and collaborate with a wide range of stakeholders. 

  • Demonstrated project management and organizational skills. 

  • Immigration VISA sponsorship will be evaluated on a case-by-case basis

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Experience with the use and application of chemical additives for oilfield related products (drilling fluids, fracturing & stimulation fluids, cement, completion fluids, production chemicals and others).

  • Formulation experience developing new products and improving existing products.

  • Prior experience with product-scale up, commercialization, or manufacturing process troubleshooting.

Physical Demands:

  • Role is deemed safety-sensitive and may be subject to employer or customer drug testing

job.description:

job.title: Regional Sales Manager
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

We are looking for a Regional Sales Manager for Windrock to create and execute regional channel strategy for its new line of wireless sensors as it applies to customers and applications. Qualified candidate may reside in Hobbs, NM, Midland, Amarillo or Lubbock, TX.

This is your opportunity to join a global technology company, driving energy innovation for a balanced planet.

What’s in it For You:

  • The ability to make an impact and shape your career with a company that is passionate about growth

  • The support of an organization that believes it is vital to include and engage diverse people, perspectives, and ideas to achieve our best

  • Thrive in a company that values sustainability, drives a safety-focused culture and empowers its employees through continuous improvement

What You Will Do:

Customer Management:

  • Identify customer’s needs, vision cast, and sales opportunities to drive growth

  • Identify and establish business relationships

  • Listen, understand, and convey customer requirements

  • Identify and leverage resources to perform technical / product training for customers at targeted accounts

  • Identify and communicate customer requirements for new products/solutions

Strategy & Planning:

  • Define a regional strategy and implement its execution for years one, two, and three

  • Identify and develop strategic account plans leveraging other channel partner (internal and external) resources

  • Drive sales through direct customer relationships, distributors, and other ChampionX entities

  • Work closely with the organization to ensure that product strategies and performance are meeting growth and Annual Operating Plan targets

  • Work closely with Marketing & Technology teams to understand pros and cons of various solution approaches

Execution:

  • Articulate value story, technology and product positioning to both business and technical users

  • Create incremental demand, opportunities, and close orders

  • Maintain expert level of product and application knowledge within the battery-operated wireless sensor market

  • Meet sales goal targets monthly, quarterly, and yearly

  • Actively manage channel partners effectiveness, prospecting, closing, forecasting, and KPI’s by using a metrics-based approach to report progress and highlight potential roadblocks with proposed mitigation strategies

  • Maintain accurate records of target accounts, opportunities, quotes, projects, contacts and correspondence in company approved databases through our Salesforce CRM

  • Other duties as assigned

Minimum Qualifications:  

  • Bachelor’s degree in engineering, business, or related field

  • Good overall knowledge of the Oil and Gas Industry

  • 5 years of experience in condition monitoring and wireless sensor applications

  • Self-driven work ethic

  • Strong negotiation skills

  • Excellent communication skills

  • Strong Interpersonal and Organizational skills

  • Strong computer skills, including Microsoft Office suite

  • Competitive attitude and strong work ethic with the ability to enthusiastically represent the company

  • Availability for inter-regional travel as required, 50-70%

  • No immigration sponsorship available for this position

    

Physical Demands

  • Specific vision abilities required by this job include close vision, distance vision, peripheral vision, depth perception and ability to adjust focus

  • The employee is regularly required to talk or hear

  • The employee is frequently required to use hands to finger, handle, or feel and reach with hands and arms

  • Ability to lift 10 lbs

job.title: Hone Operator
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

We are looking for a Hone Operator - 2nd shift located in Crowley, TX. This is your opportunity to join a growing company offering a competitive base salary and benefits.

What’s in it For You:

  • The ability to make an impact and shape your career with a company that is passionate about growth

  • The support of an organization that believes it is vital to include and engage diverse people, perspectives, and ideas to achieve our best

  • Thrive in a company that values sustainability, drives a safety-focused culture and empowers its employees through continuous improvement

  • Comprehensive benefits package that includes medical, dental, vision, 401(k) company matching and more!

Position Details:

  • 4 day - 10hr shifts, Monday - Thursday, 4:15pm to 3:00am

What You Will Do:

  • Produce barrels through utilization of saws, straighteners, CNC equipment, hones, paint equipment, and inspection processes while following standard work methods to meet customer expectations in accordance with all quality and safety protocols.

  • Learn, adhere, and apply principles of Lean Manufacturing to provide and maintain a high-quality standard

  • Learn about and participate in accomplishing Harbison-Fischer’s strategy through participation in daily team meetings

  • Participate in Continuous Improvement Program

  • Understand and operate all machines and equipment within the department

  • Cross-train on multiple pieces of equipment and processes to maximize capabilities and productivity

  • Rotate as needed among all necessary processes

  • Assist other team members to produce what is needed, when it is needed as determined by customer demand

  • Use hand-held measuring devices such as micrometers, calipers, and tape measures to inspect and measure parts for accuracy and conformance to product specifications

  • Perform repetitive motions throughout process

  • Stay on task to complete activities

  • Participate in continuous training in Lean, Kaizen, PDCA, and other principles of Operational Excellence

  • Lead or participate in PDCAs as assigned or identified

  • Work Overtime as needed

  • Perform all other duties as assigned

Pre-Employment Requirements:

  • Drug and alcohol test

  • Criminal background check

  • Education and certification check

  • Employment verification

Minimum Qualifications:

  • High school diploma or GED or equivalent education and/or experience.

  • 1 year of relevant experience.

  • Prior experience in a manufacturing environment.

  • Good computer skills

  • Excellent organizational and communication skills

  • Ability to multi-task

  • Commitment to work overtime as needed

  • Maintain appropriate levels of daily attendance, initiative and productivity

  • Read and follow detailed instructions and procedures to complete work according to proper product specifications

  • Read and understand product specifications, drawings, and prints

Physical Demands:  

  • Ability to lift up to 50 pounds and standing or walking in the work area for most of the day is a requirement of the job.

  • Specific vision abilities required by this job include close vision, distance vision, peripheral vision, depth perception and ability to adjust focus.

  • The employee is regularly required to talk or hear.

  • The employee is frequently required to use hands and fingers to pick, feel, and/or inspect material. The employee will also reach with arms and hands as needed.

  • Role is deemed safety sensitive and may be subject to employer or customer drug testing.

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Experience in the oil and gas industry preferred

  • Basic computer skills, including Microsoft Office suite - Picks up on technical things quickly; can learn new skills and knowledge is good at learning new industry, company, product, or technical knowledge; does well in technical courses and seminars.

job.title: Gas Lift Service Supervisor
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

We are looking for a Field Service Supervisor located in Tyler, TX. This is your opportunity to join a global technology company, driving energy innovation for a balanced planet.

What’s In It for You:

  • The support of an organization that believes it is vital to include and engage diverse people, perspectives, and ideas to achieve our best

  • Thrive in a company that values sustainability, drives a safety-focused culture, and empowers through continuous improvement

  • Comprehensive benefits package that includes medical, dental, vision, 401(k) with company matching and more!

What You Will Do:

  • Manages Service Technicians ensuring safety, quality, and efficient operations.

  • Schedules Service Technicians and controls field-related costs, auditing field reports (time, mileage, and quality); prepares work schedules and assigns work to maintain and manage technician productivity.

  • Develops and/or reviews quotes and repair plans based on length of repair and parts and labor availability; communicates quotes, repair plans, status reports, and invoices to customers; develops claims as required as part of the quoting procedure.

  • Manages Service vehicle inventory.  Allocates available inventory in a manner that maximizes the satisfied customer base while minimizing the probability of stock-out conditions.

  • Creates and analyzes product failure analysis reports.

  • Develops strong relationships with key customers through frequent and professional communication.

  • Knows and enforces compliance with all safety policies and procedures.

  • Drives and contributes to the department continuous improvement culture.

  • Coaches and mentor's direct reports; assigns work according to availability, skills, and developmental needs; assesses performance and provides feedback to direct reports.

  • Maintains on-going communications with Regional Operations Managers and Sales team to better understand customers’ needs and to improve the quality of our services.

  • Other duties as required.

Minimum Qualifications:

  • High School Diploma or GED

  • Two-year technical degree or equivalent education and/or experience. 

  • Valid driver's license in state of residence required.

  • Four years of relevant experience in the oil and gas industry.

  • Artificial Lift field services experience

  • Two years' experience leading field services teams.

  • Strong troubleshooting skills; ability to problem solving effectively.

  • Strong mechanical skills

  • Strong communication skills both verbal and written, ability to comminate with all levels of employees, and customers.

  • Strong safety focus; ability to lead by e

Physical Demands:

  • Must be able to lift and carry 25 lbs regularly, and 50 lbs. occasionally.

  • Specific vision abilities required by this job include close vision, distance vision, peripheral vision, depth perception and ability to adjust focus.

  • The employee is regularly required to talk or hear.

  • The employee is frequently required to use hands to finger, handle, or feel and reach with hands and arms.

  • Employee is required to occasionally walk in customer field locations which may include inclines, uneven surfaces, and a variety of weather conditions.

  • Employee must be able to regularly bend, stoop, crawl, lay down, twist and push.

  • Employee is required to wear appropriate personal protective equipment including but not limited to  shoes/boots, hard hats, or respirators.

job.title: Quality Control Inspector
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

We are looking for a Quality Control Inspector located in Houston, TX. This is your opportunity to join a global technology company, driving energy innovation for a balanced planet.


What’s In It For You:

  • The ability to make an impact and shape your career with a company that is passionate about growth

  • The support of an organization that believes it is vital to include and engage diverse people, perspectives, and ideas to achieve our best

  • Thrive in a company that values sustainability, drives a safety-focused culture and empowers its employees through continuous improvement

  • The chance to Improve Lives in our community with regular outreach and volunteerism opportunities

  • The chance to join a learning and problem-solving culture

  • Comprehensive benefits package that includes medical, dental, vision, 401(k) with company matching and more!


What You Will Do:

  • Interpret and follow blueprints, diagrams, and technical instructions for inspections.

  • Inspect materials, parts, assemblies, and sub-assemblies according  to drawings and specifications

  • Communicate quality control issues and recommend changes to enhance product quality

  • Perform routine checks on red tag bins in the production areas to ensure established NCR procedures are followed.

  • Perform incoming inspection of purchased product in accordance with industry standard and AQL sampling plan

  • Assure that manufactured product meet quality control standards, engineering specifications and customer requirements

  • Participate in investigation and disposition of customer complaints, nonconforming products and returned goods

  • Read and interpret material test reports and heat treat / mechanical certs

  • Demonstrate a personable, positive, and enthusiastic attitude

  • Report unsafe acts or conditions to supervision immediately as noticed

  • Initiate corrective action requests when conditions are averse to quality

  • Complete, inspect and distribute all quality records and documents, as necessary

  • Participate in Lean events with active involvement in CI initiatives

  • Understand and follow verbal and written instruction


Pre-Employment Requirements:

  • Drug & Alcohol screening

  • Criminal background review

  • Education & Employment history review


Position Details:

  • Candidate must reside in: Houston TX, or surrounding areas

  • Work week and shift: 6AM-2:30PM, M – F, some daily overtime and weekend work may be occasionally required

  • Compensation package offered: Competitive hourly rate and quarterly bonus eligibility

  • New hire orientation is provided

  • Training provided on processes, procedures, and product assembly

  • PPE requirements may include safety glasses, steel toed shoes, gloves, etc.

  • Work location is in an enclosed shop quality lab environment with an open workspace

  • Immigration sponsorship is not provided for this position


Minimum Qualifications:

  • 3 years’ experience in quality inspection within a manufacturing environment

  • Experience in an Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) system and Quality Management Software (QMS)

  • Must be skilled in using inspection equipment including Calipers, Mic’s, Bore gauges, Optical Comparator, Hardness Testers, Height Gage etc.

  • Self-starter, highly motivated, able to work with limited supervision

  • Must be able to read and interpret material certs and heat treat / mechanical certs

  • Knowledge of assembly inspection, testing and manufacturing

  • Knowledge of Machine Shop In-process Inspection, ECN Process and GD&T

  • Knowledge of quality standards and manufacturing processes

  • Must be able to work independently and within a team environment with a high degree of accuracy, strong organization skill as well as excellent attention to detail

  • Proficient with Microsoft Office Suite

  • Show respect, understanding, tact and concern to others; develops and maintains effective relationships with others

  • Maintain confidentiality and has high ethical standards 

  • Demonstrate a personable, positive, and enthusiastic attitude

  • Foster teamwork and collaboration within and across work groups

  • Is open to change and new information; adapts behavior or work methods in response to new information, changing conditions or unexpected obstacles; effectively deals with ambiguity

  • Must have a professional and positive attitude as well as a passion for continuous improvement

  • Understanding of current process requirements, rules, and procedures

Physical Demands:

  • Role is deemed safety sensitive and may be subject to employer or customer drug testing.

  • Minimal bending, crouching, kneeling, squatting, climbing stairs, reaching, twisting, stretching, pushing, pulling, collating/filing, and dialing.

  • Occasional standing and hearing.

  • Frequent lifting between 25lbs to 40lbs, walking and writing.

  • Constant lifting up to 10lbs, handling, holding, grasping, turning, working with hands, fingering, picking, pinching, fine manipulation, using a keyboard and mouse, viewing a computer monitor and small parts and products, sitting, seeing and speaking.

  • Work environment includes temperature-controlled shop floor, quality lab, front offices and conference rooms.

    Preferred Qualifications:

    • Experience in Oracle ERP

    • Experience in Entropy QMS

    • Experience in Adobe

    • Knowledgeable of Industry Standard Requirements (ANSI, ASME, ISO 9001, etc.)

    • Operate and program CMM (knowledge or FARO/ROMER arm a plus)

    About Us:

    ChampionX is now part of SLB, a global technology company driving energy innovation for a balanced planet. As innovators, that’s been our mission for nearly a century.

job.title: Principal Chemist
job.description:
SLB has an immediate need for a highly motivated Principal Chemist in our New Product Development team located in Sugar Land, Texas. The Principal Chemist will support strategic innovation projects for Chemically Enhanced Oil Recovery and Production Enhancement. The Principal Chemist will also support manufacturing and supply chain to scale up products from lab to plant. The ideal candidate is highly versatile, communicates effectively, and thrives in a collaborative environment.

If you are a passionate professional that likes to work in an autonomous, hands-on environment, we invite you to apply. This is your opportunity to join a growing company offering a competitive base salary and benefits.

What’s in it For You:

  • The ability to make an impact and shape your career with a company that is passionate about growth.

  • The support of an organization that believes it is vital to include and engage diverse people, perspectives and ideas to achieve our best.

  • Thrive in a company that values sustainability, drives a safety-focused culture and has been recognized as one of the most ethical companies in the world.

  • Comprehensive benefits package starting day 1 of employment- medical, dental, vision, matching 401k, and more!

What You Will Do:

  • Perform work in a safe manner while identifying opportunities for continuous improvement.

  • Conduct applied scientific research aimed at solving complex problems related to Chemically Enhanced Oil Recovery.

  • Execute strategic innovation projects with possible global reach and high impact on the business.

  • Develop novel chemistries/formulations to meet customer needs

  • Develop new testing and/or dispensing methods.

  • Analyze research data and prepare detailed reports and presentations

  • Drive the continuous improvement of our business to responsibly deliver energy.

  • Communicate technical recommendations or test results to a wide range of audiences including internal and external customers

  • Work effectively in a team environment with cross-functional members.

  • Monitor and track state-of-the-art trends across assigned technical domains.

Minimum Qualifications:

  • MSc in Chemistry, Chemical Engineering, Materials Engineering or related fields with more than four years of relevant work experience or PhD with less than three years of work experience in Chemistry, Chemical Engineering, Materials Engineering or related fields.

  • Experience in organic synthesis and/or polymerization

  • Understanding of structure/properties relationship.

  • Knowledge of interfacial science (IFT, contact angle, surface tension)

  • Ability to work with business counterparts to support our customers and new technology adoption.

  • Ability to analyze and interpret incomplete information.

  • Ability to operate independently and as a part of a highly effective team.

  • Demonstrate expertise and breadth of knowledge to solve challenges where precedents typically do not exist

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Prior experience in latex polymerization reactions.

  • Experience in Enhanced Oil Recovery is a plus.

  • Strong problem-solving skills and track record of successful innovation in the form of conference papers and/or patents.

  • Product development experience with taking a product from lab scale to commercial scale.

  • Experience in formal Design of Experiments and/or Data Analytics is a plus

  • Detail orientated and excellent time management skills

  • Excellent written, oral, and visual communication skills to communicate effectively with all levels of the organization, both within and outside the business

Physical Demands:

  • Role is deemed safety-sensitive and may be subject to employer or customer drug testing

About Us

ChampionX is now part of SLB, a global technology company driving energy innovation for a balanced planet. As innovators, that’s been our mission for nearly a century. We are a technology company that unlocks access to energy for the benefit of all. As innovators, that’s been our mission for nearly a century. Today, we face a global imperative to create a future with more energy, but less carbon. Our diverse, innovative change makers are focused on going further in innovation and inventing the new energy technologies we need to get there. For more news and information, visit SLB.com. job.title: District Representative II
job.description:
ChampionX has an immediate need for a District Representative II in Lafayette, LA. This is your opportunity to join a large growing company offering a competitive base salary, bonus structure and benefits.

What’s in it For You:

  • You will join a growing company offering competitive pay and comprehensive benefits package that includes medical, dental, vision, 401(k) with company matching and more!

  • Access to best-in-class resources, tools, and technology

  • Opportunity for a long term, advanced career path

  • A culture that values safety first, including training and personal protection

What You Will Do:

  • Work closely with current and new customers to understand business needs and recommend continuous improvement and innovation plans that will maintain and grow sales within assigned territory

  • Assist in performing customer service and account management activities under the guidance and supervision of experienced sales professionals

  • Provide technical support to customers; identifying and resolving customer complaints, escalating as required

  • Engage in problem solving by performing system analysis, interpreting data and providing written recommendations to ensure customer operations are performing at optimal levels.

  • Actively sell and support ChampionX innovations and technology in assigned customers to promote long-term business relationships with ChampionX

  • Assist in developing strong relationships with key stakeholders in current and new customers, including plant or facility executives

  • Achieve annual sales target by working collaboratively on targeting new business opportunities through application of professional selling and planning techniques

  • Assist with collection of samples and conduct laboratory analysis

Position Details:

  • Up to 25% overnight travel required to support sales territory

  • Ability to travel within assigned sales territory

Minimum Qualifications:

  • High School or GED Equivalent

  • Must have a valid Driver’s License and acceptable Motor Vehicle Record

  • Must be 21 years of age or older to operate a company vehicle

  • Immigration sponsorship not offered for this role  

Physical Demands:

  • May be required to Lift/Push/Pull/Carry up to 55 pounds chest high.

  • Role is deemed safety-sensitive and may be subject to employer or customer drug testing.

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Engineering (Chemical, Mechanical, Industrial), or Life Sciences (Biology, Chemistry, etc.)

  • 2-3 years of successful technical sales or field sales support experience

  • Experience in oil and gas industry

  • High computer application literacy (including Microsoft Office Suite and ability to learn internal business systems)

  • History of working in a field that required autonomy and self-motivation

  • Prior experience that required excellent communication and organizational skills

  • Prior experience that demonstrates strong ethics, effective time management, ability to multitask, adaptability, autonomy and self-motivation

About ChampionX

ChampionX is a global leader in providing chemistry programs and services, drilling technology, artificial lift solutions, and automation technologies for the upstream and midstream oil and gas industry. Our world-class safety culture fuels our purpose to improve lives through our commitment to deliver sustainable operations.

job.title: Field Service Technician Trainee
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

We are looking for a Field Service Technician Trainee located in Odessa, TX. This is your opportunity to join a global technology company, driving energy innovation for a balanced planet.

What’s in it For You:

  • You will join a growing company offering competitive pay and benefits

  • Access to best-in-class resources, tools, and technology

  • Opportunity for a long term, advanced career path

  • A culture that values safety first, including training and personal protection

What You Will Do:

  • Works under direct supervision to perform basic customer support activities.

  • Assists higher level technicians with on-site installations, check-out, and emergency repairs.

  • Refers more complex problems to higher level technicians or supervisor.

Minimum Qualifications:

  • Associates Degree in Electronics, Automation, or related field or related work experience

  • Basic knowledge of industrial safety

  • Basic knowledge of proper use of hand tools

  • Basic knowledge of wiring and cabling

  • Ability to execute strategies and initiatives

  • Analytical and problem-solving ability

  • Intermediate oral and written communication skills

  • Organization and time management skills

  • Basic math skills

  • Ability to follow prescribed company policies, procedures, and protocols for installation, service and equipment maintenance

  • Interact politely and professionally with customers and/or coworkers

  • Ability to effectively present information and respond to questions from managers, clients, and customers

  • Ability to effectively plan and organize

  • Follow all safety policies and company-wide safety requirements; encourage action assuring safe behavior; confront unsafe behavior and conditions proactively and positively

  • Work in excess of 8 hours per day and 40 hours per week, as required to meet business needs

  • Work in an unsupervised environment

  • Read, write, and speak English fluently

  • Use a computer to communicate, create, and access data

  • Determine the priorities, goals, and objectives of work assigned

  • Immigration sponsorship not offered for this role

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Experience working with ESP’s

  • Experience working with VFD’s and transformers

Physical Demands:

  • Role is deemed safety-sensitive and may be subject to employer or customer drug testing.

  • The physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job. 

  • Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.

  • Exerting up to 50 pounds of force occasionally and/or up to 20 pounds of force frequently, and/or up to 10 pounds of force constantly to move objects.

  • Ascending or descending ladders, stairs, scaffolding, ramps, poles, and the like, using feet and legs and/or hands and arms. Body agility is emphasized.

  • Maintaining body equilibrium to prevent falling when walking, standing, or crouching on narrow, slippery, or erratically moving surfaces.

  • Bending body downward and forward by bending spine at the waist. This factor is important if it occurs to a considerable degree and requires full use of the lower extremities and back muscles.

  • Bending legs at knee to come to a rest on knees.

  • Bending the body downward and forward by bending leg and spine.

  • Moving about on hands and knees or hands and feet.

  • Extending hand(s) and arm(s) in any direction.

  • Moving about on foot to accomplish tasks, particularly for long distances or moving from one work site to another.

  • Using upper extremities to press against something with steady force in order to thrust forward, downward, or outward.

  • Using upper extremities to exert force in order to draw, drag, haul, or tug objects in a sustained motion.

  • Raising objects from a lower to a higher position or moving objects horizontally from position to position.

  • Picking, pinching, typing, or otherwise working, primarily with fingers rather than the whole hand or arm as in handling.

  • Applying pressure to an object with the fingers and palm.

  • Perceiving attributes of objects, such as size, shape, temperature, or texture by touching with skin, particularly that of fingertips.

  • Expressing or exchanging ideas by means of the spoken word.  Those activities in which they must convey detailed or important spoken instructions to customer or other workers accurately, loudly, or quickly.

  • Perceiving the nature of sounds at normal speaking levels with or without correction.  Ability to receive detailed information through oral communication, and to make the discriminations in sound.

  • Substantial movement (motions) of the wrists, hands, and/or fingers to use a computer.

  • The employee is required to have close visual acuity to perform an activity such as: visual inspection involving small defects, small parts and/or operation of machines (including inspection), using measurement devices, and/or assembly or fabrication of parts at distances close to the eyes.

  • The employee is required to have visual acuity to operate motor vehicles.

  • The employee is required to have visual acuity to determine the accuracy, neatness, thoroughness of work assigned or to make general observations of facilities or structures.

  • Indoor and outdoor environmental conditions.  Activities occur indoors and outdoors.

  • Extreme heat.  Temperatures above 100 degrees for periods of more than one hour.  Consideration should be given to the effect of other environmental conditions, such as wind and humidity.

  • Hazards.  Includes a variety of physical conditions, such as proximity to moving mechanical parts, moving vehicles, electrical current or exposure to chemicals.

  • Atmospheric conditions.  One or more of the following conditions that affect the respiratory system or the skin:  fumes, odors, dust, mists, gases, or poor ventilation.

  • Oils. There is air and/or skin exposure to oils and other cutting fluids.
